member.name: T:UnrealBuildTool.ActionType
member.summary: Enumerates build action types.

member.name: T:UnrealBuildTool.ArtifactMode
member.summary: Defines the action's support for artifacts

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.ArtifactMode.None
member.summary: Cached artifacts aren't enabled

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.ArtifactMode.Enabled
member.summary: If set, the outputs should be cached but respect the flags below

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.ArtifactMode.AbsolutePath
member.summary: Absolute file paths must be used when recording inputs and outputs

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.ArtifactMode.PropagateInputs
member.summary: For actions that can't be cached, by setting this flag, the inputs for the action in question will be used as additional inputs for any action that references outputs of this action. For example, PCH files shouldn't be cached, but compiles that use the PCH should still be valid if consider the inputs used to create the PCH file.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.IExternalAction.ActionType
member.summary: The type of this action (for debugging purposes).

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.IExternalAction.ArtifactMode
member.summary: Artifact support for this step

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.IExternalAction.PrerequisiteItems
member.summary: Every file this action depends on. These files need to exist and be up to date in order for this action to even be considered

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.IExternalAction.ProducedItems
member.summary: The files that this action produces after completing

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.IExternalAction.DeleteItems
member.summary: Items that should be deleted before running this action

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.IExternalAction.RootPaths
member.summary: Root paths for this action (generally engine root project root, toolchain root, sdk root)

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.IExternalAction.DependencyListFile
member.summary: For C++ source files, specifies a dependency list file used to check changes to header files

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.IExternalAction.WorkingDirectory
member.summary: Directory from which to execute the program to create produced items

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.IExternalAction.CommandPath
member.summary: The command to run to create produced items

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.IExternalAction.CommandArguments
member.summary: Command-line parameters to pass to the program

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.IExternalAction.ResponseFileContents
member.summary: Any arguments used in a response file to specify arguments to the executable. The response file is expected to be passed as part of .

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.IExternalAction.CommandVersion
member.summary: Version of the command used for this action. This will be considered a dependency.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.IExternalAction.CommandDescription
member.summary: Optional friendly description of the type of command being performed, for example "Compile" or "Link". Displayed by some executors.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.IExternalAction.StatusDescription
member.summary: Human-readable description of this action that may be displayed as status while invoking the action. This is often the name of the file being compiled, or an executable file name being linked. Displayed by some executors.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.IExternalAction.bCanExecuteRemotely
member.summary: True if this action is allowed to be run on a remote machine when a distributed build system is being used, such as XGE

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.IExternalAction.bCanExecuteRemotelyWithSNDBS
member.summary: True if this action is allowed to be run on a remote machine with SNDBS. Files with #import directives must be compiled locally. Also requires bCanExecuteRemotely = true.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.IExternalAction.bCanExecuteRemotelyWithXGE
member.summary: True if this action is allowed to be run on a remote machine with XGE. Files with #import directives must be compiled locally. Also requires bCanExecuteRemotely = true.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.IExternalAction.bCanExecuteInUBA
member.summary: True if this action can be executed by Unreal Build Accelerator

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.IExternalAction.bCanExecuteInUBACrossArchitecture
member.summary: True if this action can be executed on a cross-architecture Unreal Build Accelerator helper. If no cross-architecture toolchain is found this setting has no effect

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.IExternalAction.bIsClangCompiler
member.summary: True if this action is using the Clang compiler. Some build systems may be able to optimize for this case.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.IExternalAction.bDeleteProducedItemsOnError
member.summary: True if the action executor should delete output files on an error. May not work for XGE, SN-DBS, and FASTBuild executors.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.IExternalAction.bForceWarningsAsError
member.summary: True if the action executor should force warning output to fail the action when the exit code would otherwise be zero. Used if the command to be run does not support a warnings as error flag. May not work for XGE, SN-DBS, and FASTBuild executors.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.IExternalAction.bShouldOutputStatusDescription
member.summary: Whether we should log this action, whether executed locally or remotely. This is useful for actions that take time but invoke tools without any console output.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.IExternalAction.bShouldOutputLog
member.summary: Whether we should output log of this action, whether executed locally or remotely. This is useful for actions that spam logs to console output with non critical data

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.IExternalAction.bProducesImportLibrary
member.summary: True if any libraries produced by this action should be considered 'import libraries'

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.IExternalAction.bUseActionHistory
member.summary: Whether changes in the command line used to generate these produced items should invalidate the action

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.IExternalAction.bIsHighPriority
member.summary: True if this action should be scheduled early. Some actions can take a long time (pch) and need to start very early If set to true it will propagate high priority to all its prerequisite tasks

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.IExternalAction.Weight
member.summary: Used to determine how much weight(CPU and Memory work) this action is.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.IExternalAction.CacheBucket
member.summary: Used to determine which cache bucket should be used for this action Using different cache buckets is an optimization where we want one build to use as few buckets as possible while two different builds should not use the same bucket if there is no chance their cache entries will be the same

member.summary: Determines whether an action is outdated by examining the up-to-date state of all of its prerequisites, recursively. Not thread safe. Typically very fast.
member.returns: true if outdated

member.name: T:UnrealBuildTool.BuildConfiguration
member.summary: Global settings for building. Should not contain any target-specific settings.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.BuildConfiguration.bIgnoreOutdatedImportLibraries
member.summary: Whether to ignore import library files that are out of date when building targets. Set this to true to improve iteration time. By default, we do not bother re-linking targets if only a dependent .lib has changed, as chances are that the import library was not actually different unless a dependent header file of this target was also changed, in which case the target would automatically be rebuilt.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.BuildConfiguration.bUsePrecompiled
member.summary: Use existing static libraries for all engine modules in this target.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.BuildConfiguration.bPrintDebugInfo
member.summary: Whether debug info should be written to the console.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.BuildConfiguration.bAllowHybridExecutor
member.summary: Whether the hybrid executor will be used (a remote executor and local executor). No longer supported.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.BuildConfiguration.RemoteExecutorPriority
member.summary: Priority order for remote executors (XGE, SNDBS, FASTBuild, UBA)

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.BuildConfiguration.bAllowUBAExecutor
member.summary: Whether the UnrealBuildAccelerator executor will be used.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.BuildConfiguration.bAllowUBALocalExecutor
member.summary: Whether the UnrealBuildAccelerator (local only) executor will be used.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.BuildConfiguration.bAllowXGE
member.summary: Whether XGE may be used if available, default is true.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.BuildConfiguration.bAllowFASTBuild
member.summary: Whether FASTBuild may be used if available, default is true.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.BuildConfiguration.bAllowSNDBS
member.summary: Whether SN-DBS may be used if available, default is true.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.BuildConfiguration.bUseUBTMakefiles
member.summary:
Enables support for very fast iterative builds by caching target data. Turning this on causes Unreal Build Tool to emit 'UBT Makefiles' for targets when they are built the first time. Subsequent builds will load these Makefiles and begin outdatedness checking and build invocation very quickly. The caveat is that if source files are added or removed to the project, UBT will need to gather information about those in order for your build to complete successfully. Currently, you must run the project file generator after adding/removing source files to tell UBT to re-gather this information.
Events that can invalidate the 'UBT Makefile': - Adding/removing .cpp files - Adding/removing .h files with UObjects - Adding new UObject types to a file that did not previously have any - Changing global build settings (most settings in this file qualify) - Changed code that affects how Unreal Header Tool works
You can force regeneration of the 'UBT Makefile' by passing the '-gather' argument, or simply regenerating project files.
This also enables the fast include file dependency scanning and caching system that allows Unreal Build Tool to detect out of date dependencies very quickly. When enabled, a deep C++ include graph does not have to be generated, and instead, we only scan and cache indirect includes for after a dependent build product was already found to be out of date. During the next build, we will load those cached indirect includes and check for outdatedness.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.BuildConfiguration.MaxParallelActions
member.summary: Number of actions that can be executed in parallel. If 0 then code will pick a default based on the number of cores and memory available. Applies to the ParallelExecutor, HybridExecutor, and LocalExecutor

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.BuildConfiguration.bAllCores
member.summary: Consider logical cores when determining how many total cpu cores are available.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.BuildConfiguration.bForceHeaderGeneration
member.summary: If true, force header regeneration. Intended for the build machine.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.BuildConfiguration.bEnableUHTInputCache
member.summary: If true, enable the UHT input cache for both reading and writing

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.BuildConfiguration.bDoNotBuildUHT
member.summary: If true, do not build UHT, assume it is already built.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.BuildConfiguration.bFailIfGeneratedCodeChanges
member.summary: If true, fail if any of the generated header files is out of date.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.BuildConfiguration.bAllowHotReloadFromIDE
member.summary: True if hot-reload from IDE is allowed.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.BuildConfiguration.bForceDebugUnrealHeaderTool
member.summary: If true, the Debug version of UnrealHeaderTool will be built and run instead of the Development version.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.BuildConfiguration.bUseBuiltInUnrealHeaderTool
member.summary: If true, use C# UHT internal to UBT

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.BuildConfiguration.bWarnOnCppUnrealHeaderTool
member.summary: If true, generate warnings when C++ UHT is used

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.BuildConfiguration.bSkipRulesCompile
member.summary: Whether to skip compiling rules assemblies and just assume they are valid

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.BuildConfiguration.bForceRulesCompile
member.summary: Whether to force compiling rules assemblies, regardless of whether they are valid

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.BuildConfiguration.MaxRootPathLength
member.summary: Maximum recommended root path length.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.BuildConfiguration.MaxNestedPathLength
member.summary: Maximum length of a path relative to the root directory. Used on Windows to ensure paths are portable between machines.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.BuildConfiguration.bIgnoreInvalidFiles
member.summary: When single file targets are specified, via -File=, -SingleFile=, or -FileList= If this option is set, no error will be produced if the source file is not included in the target. Additionally, if any file or file list is specified, the target will not be built if none of the specified files are part of that target, including the case where a file specified via -FileList= is empty.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.PluginDescriptor.Version
member.summary: Version number for the plugin. The version number must increase with every version of the plugin, so that the system can determine whether one version of a plugin is newer than another, or to enforce other requirements. This version number is not displayed in front-facing UI. Use the VersionName for that.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.PluginDescriptor.VersionName

member.summary: When true, prevents other plugins from depending on this plugin

member.summary: When true, this plugin should not contain any code or modules.

member.summary: If true, the GameFeaturePlugin this is on should have no content other than the GameFeatureData asset.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.PluginDescriptor.bExplicitlyLoaded

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.PluginDescriptor.bHasExplicitPlatforms

member.summary: Container class used for C++ compiler warning settings.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.ShadowVariableWarningLevel
member.summary: Forces shadow variable warnings to be treated as errors on platforms that support it. MSVC - https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cpp/error-messages/compiler-warnings/compiler-warning-level-4-c4456 4456 - declaration of 'LocalVariable' hides previous local declaration https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cpp/error-messages/compiler-warnings/compiler-warning-level-4-c4458 4458 - declaration of 'parameter' hides class member https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cpp/error-messages/compiler-warnings/compiler-warning-level-4-c4459 4459 - declaration of 'LocalVariable' hides global declaration Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wshadow

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.UnsafeTypeCastWarningLevel
member.summary:
Indicates what warning/error level to treat unsafe type casts as on platforms that support it (e.g., double->float or int64->int32) MSVC - https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cpp/error-messages/compiler-warnings/compiler-warning-levels-3-and-4-c4244 4244 - conversion from 'type1' to 'type2', possible loss of data 44244 - Note: The extra 4 is not a typo, /wLXXXX sets warning XXXX to level L https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cpp/error-messages/compiler-warnings/compiler-warning-level-1-c4838 4838 - conversion from 'type1' to 'type2' requires a narrowing conversion 44838 - Note: The extra 4 is not a typo, /wLXXXX sets warning XXXX to level L Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wfloat-conversion https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wimplicit-float-conversion https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wimplicit-int-conversion https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wc-11-narrowing
To enable (too many hits right now) https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wshorten-64-to-32 https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wsign-conversion
member.remarks: This should be kept in sync with PRAGMA_DISABLE_UNSAFE_TYPECAST_WARNINGS in ClangPlatformCompilerPreSetup.h
Clang: "shorten-64-to-32"; too many hits right now, probably want it *soon*; "sign-conversion"; too many hits right now, probably want it eventually

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.UndefinedIdentifierWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat undefined identifiers in conditional expressions. MSVC - https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cpp/error-messages/compiler-warnings/compiler-warning-level-4-c4668 4668 - 'symbol' is not defined as a preprocessor macro, replacing with '0' for 'directives' 44668 - Note: The extra 4 is not a typo, /wLXXXX sets warning XXXX to level L Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wundef

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.UnreachableCodeWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat unreachable code. MSVC - https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cpp/error-messages/compiler-warnings/compiler-warning-level-4-c4702 4702 - unreachable code 44702 - Note: The extra 4 is not a typo, /wLXXXX sets warning XXXX to level L Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wunreachable-code-aggressive

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.SwitchUnhandledEnumeratorWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat unhandled enumerators in switches on enumeration-typed values. MSVC - 4061 - https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cpp/error-messages/compiler-warnings/compiler-warning-level-4-c4061 44061 - Note: The extra 4 is not a typo, /wLXXXX sets warning XXXX to level L Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wswitch-enum

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.ShortenSizeTToIntWarningLevel
member.summary: The compiler detected a conversion from size_t to a smaller type. MSVC - https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cpp/error-messages/compiler-warnings/compiler-warning-level-3-c4267 4267 - 'var' : conversion from 'size_t' to 'type', possible loss of data

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.DeleteNonVirtualDtorWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat invocations to destructors that aren't virtual. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wdelete-non-virtual-dtor

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.EnumConversionWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat implicit conversions betwen enums and integers. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wenum-conversion

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.ClangBitfieldEnumConversion
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat assignments of enum types to a bit=field, which can lead to implicit conversations (causing unexpected results). Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wbitfield-enum-conversion

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.EnumEnumConversionWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat conversions between enum types. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wenum-enum-conversion

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.EnumFloatConversionWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat conversions between an enumeration and a floating-point type. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wenum-float-conversion

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.AmbiguousReversedOperatorWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat ambiguous use of reversed operator overloads. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wambiguous-reversed-operator

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.DeprecatedAnonEnumEnumConversionWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat implicit conversions between two different anonymous enumerations Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wdeprecated-anon-enum-enum-conversion

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.DeprecatedVolatileWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat deprecated usages of volatile (due to better alternatives like atomic operations). Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wdeprecated-volatile

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.OrderedCompareFunctionPointers
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat compairing function pointers with relational operators (<,>, etc), which is undefined behavior. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wordered-compare-function-pointers

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.BitwiseInsteadOfLogicalWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat bitwise operations are used where logical operators are likely intended. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wbitwise-instead-of-logical

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.DeprecatedCopyWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat usages of a class where a deprecated copy constructor or copy assignment operator exists. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wdeprecated-copy

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.DeprecatedCopyWithUserProvidedCopyWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat usages of a class where a deprecated implicitly declared copy constructor or copy assignment operator exists. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wdeprecated-copy-with-user-provided-copy
member.remarks: due to it's relation to .

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.InvalidUnevaluatedStringWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat invalid, unevaluated strings. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#winvalid-unevaluated-string

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.NaNInfinityDisabledWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat infinity macros, and subsequent undefined behavior. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wnan-infinity-disabled
member.remarks: We use the NAN macro in a few places to initialize floats while we also set -ffast-math, which disables NaN support. It could be easily fixed in the engine, but would create the risk of Win64 building fine and the code failing just on Clang-based platforms. We tend to use NAN just as a bit pattern, and not rely on it in calculations, so it should be reasonably safe to disable.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.LevelExtraQualificationWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat extra qualifications. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wextra-qualification

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.CastFunctionTypeMismatchWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat function pointer casts to an incompatible function type, potentially leading to undefined behavior. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wcast-function-type-mismatch

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.MissingTemplateArgListAfterTemplateWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat templates used without specifying the required template arguments, such as missing parameter packs (<...>) after the template keyword. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wmissing-template-arg-list-after-template-kw

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.GNUStringLiteralOperatorTemplateWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat gnu string literal operator templates. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wgnu-string-literal-operator-template
member.remarks: We use this feature to allow static FNames.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.InconsistentMissingOverrideWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat inconsistent missing overrides. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#winconsistent-missing-override

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.InvalidOffsetWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat incorrect usages of std::offsetof, such as with non-POD types, where behavior is undefined. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#winvalid-offsetof
member.remarks: Needed to suppress warnings about using offsetof on non-POD types.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.SwitchWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat switch related warnings. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wswitch
member.remarks: This hides the "enumeration value 'XXXXX' not handled in switch [-Wswitch]" warnings - we should maybe remove this at some point and add UE_LOG(, Fatal, ) to default cases.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.TautologicalCompareWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat tautological comparisons (i.e. obvious comparisons that are always guaranteed). Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wtautological-compare
member.remarks: This hides the "warning : comparison of unsigned expression < 0 is always false" type warnings due to constant comparisons, which are possible with template arguments/

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.UnknownPragmasWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat unknown pragmas with. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wunknown-pragmas
member.remarks: Slate triggers this (with its optimize on/off pragmas).

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.UnusedWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat unused symbols. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wunused-but-set-variable https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wunused-but-set-parameter https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wunused-function https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wunused-lambda-capture https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wunused-local-typedef https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wunused-private-field https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wunused-variable
member.remarks: unused-function: This will hide the warnings about static functions in headers that aren't used in every single .cpp file. unused-lambda-capture: Suppressed because capturing of compile-time constants is seemingly inconsistent. And MSVC doesn't do that. unused-local-typedef: Clang is being overly strict here? PhysX headers trigger this. unused-private-field: This will prevent the issue of warnings for unused private variables. MultichannelTcpSocket.h triggers this, possibly more.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.UndefinedVarTemplateWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat undefined templates for variables. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wundefined-var-template
member.remarks: Not a good warning to be disabling.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.ProfileInstructWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat profile instructions. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wprofile-instr-out-of-date https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wprofile-instr-unprofiled
member.remarks: Clang emits warnings for each compiled object file that doesn't have a matching entry in the profile data. This can happen when the profile data is older than the binaries we're compiling. Disable these warnings.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.BackendPluginWarningLevel
member.summary: Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wbackend-plugin
member.remarks: Apparently there can be hashing conflicts with PGO which can result in: 'Function control flow change detected (hash mismatch)' warnings.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.UnusedValueWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat values that are computed, but not used (i.e.unnecessary evaluation of an rvalue or expression). Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wunused-value
member.remarks: Shipping builds will cause -Wno-unused-value to warn with "ensure", so disable only in those case. -Wunused-result - we will always re-enable this as it's controlled by the same set. This will be moved out to a separate property. It is on by default, so it only must be re-enabled in the disable context.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.Shorten64To32WarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat implicit conversaion losses for integer precision. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wshorten-64-to-32
member.remarks: -Wimplicit-int-conversion also controls -Wshorten-64-to-32 as of clang 19.
due to it's relation to .

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.DllExportExplicitInstantiationDeclWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat incorrect extern template declarations. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wdllexport-explicit-instantiation-decl
member.remarks: The code base contains lots of places where we do "extern template X_API class ..." and we want to keep doing that to reduce compile times.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.NonTrivialMemAccessWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat nontrivial memory access. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wnontrivial-memaccess

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.MicrosoftGroupWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat warnings associated with Microsoft-specific language extensions and compatibility issues. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wmicrosoft
member.remarks: Allow Microsoft-specific syntax to slide, even though it may be non-standard. Needed for Windows headers.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.MSVCIncludeWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat compatibility mods for clang, and how it searches for system headers. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wmsvc-include
member.remarks: Hack due to how we have our 'DummyPCH' wrappers setup when using unity builds. This warning should not be disabled!

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.PragmaPackWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat improper or inconsistent usage of #pragma pack, which can lead to ABI/unexpected struct layouts. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wpragma-pack
member.remarks: This is disabled because clang explicitly warns about changing pack alignment in a header and not restoring it afterwards, which is something we do with the Pre/PostWindowsApi.h headers. @TODO clang: push/pop this in Pre/PostWindowsApi.h headers instead?

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.InlineNewDeleteWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat cases where operator new or operator delete is declared inline, leading to issues with memory allocdealloc across different translation units. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#winline-new-delete
member.remarks: @todo clang: We declare operator new as inline. Clang doesn't seem to like that.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.ImplicitExceptionSpecMismatchWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat cases where function declarations and definitions have mismatched implicit exception qualifiers. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wimplicit-exception-spec-mismatch

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.UndefinedBoolConversionWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat cases when an expression that isn't explicitly a boolean is used in a boolean context. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wundefined-bool-conversion
member.remarks: Sometimes we compare 'this' pointers against nullptr, which Clang warns about by default.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.DeprecatedWriteableStringsWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat usages of string literals as writable char* (allowed in older c++), but has since been deprecated. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wdeprecated-writable-strings

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.DeprecatedRegisterWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat deprecated usages of the register storage class specifier. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wdeprecated-register

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.LogicalOpParenthesesWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat usages of logical operations and related parenthesis use. e.g. return false && false || array[f.get()]; // expected-warning {{'&&' within '||'}} expected-note {{parentheses}} Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wlogical-op-parentheses
member.remarks: Disable needed for external headers we shan't change.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.NullArithmeticWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat occurrences where arithmetic operations are performed on nullptr. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wnull-arithmetic
member.remarks: Disable needed for external headers we shan't change.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.ReturnTypeCLinkageWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat cases where a function with C linkage has a return type that isn't compatible with C. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wreturn-type-c-linkage
member.remarks: Disable needed for PhysX.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.IgnoredAttributesWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat usages of attributes applied to a function or variable that are ignored. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wignored-attributes
member.remarks: Disable needed for nvtesslib.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.UninitializedWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat varaibles that are used before initialization. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wuninitialized

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.ReturnTypeWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat functions with a non-void return type, but doesn't return a value in all code paths. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wreturn-type
member.remarks: Disable needed for external headers we shan't change.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.UnusedParameterWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat function parameters that are declared, but not used within the function body. MSVC - https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cpp/error-messages/compiler-warnings/compiler-warning-level-4-c4100 4100 - 'identifier' : unreferenced formal parameter Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wunused-parameter
member.remarks: Unused function parameter. A lot are named 'bUnused'...

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.IgnoredQualifiersWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat type qualifiers (const/volatile) that are applied to a pointer or ref, but are ignored due to not having an effect. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wignored-qualifiers
member.remarks: const ignored when returning by value e.g. 'const int foo() { return 4; }'.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.ExpansionToDefined
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat occurrences of macro expansion resulting in a defined expression being used in an undefined manner. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wexpansion-to-defined
member.remarks: Usage of 'defined(X)' in a macro definition. Gives different results under MSVC.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.SignCompareWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat comparisons of signed and unsigned integer types. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wsign-compare
member.remarks: Signed/unsigned comparison - millions of these.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.MissingFieldInitializersWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat initializer lists used for structor or array missing one or more fields. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wmissing-field-initializers
member.remarks: Excessive warning, generated when you initialize with MyStruct A = {0};

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.NonPortableIncludePathWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat usages of non-portable include paths. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wnonportable-include-path

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.InvalidTokenPasteWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat preprocessor's token-pasting operator (##) is used incorrectly, resulting in ill-formed tokens after concatenation. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#winvalid-token-paste MASVC - https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cpp/preprocessor/token-pasting-operator-hash-hash?view=msvc-170

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.NullPointerArithmeticWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat arithmetic operations that are performed with a null pointer. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wnull-pointer-arithmetic

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.ConstantLogicalOperandWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat usages of constant values being used within a logical operation, which may lead to redundant logic evaluation. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wconstant-logical-operand
member.remarks: Triggered by || of two template-derived values inside a static_assert.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.BitfieldEnumConversion
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat assignments of enum types to a bit=field, which can lead to implicit conversations (causing unexpected results). Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wbitfield-enum-conversion

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.NullPointerSubtractionWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat instances of pointer subtraction, where a pointer may be null. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wnull-pointer-subtraction

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.DanglingWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat dangling pointer usage. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wdangling

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.MSVCSwitchEnumWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat unhandled enumerators in switches on enumeration-typed values. MSVC - 4061 - https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cpp/error-messages/compiler-warnings/compiler-warning-level-4-c4061 44061 - Note: The extra 4 is not a typo, /wLXXXX sets warning XXXX to level L Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wswitch-enum
member.remarks: This is a special case where we have a duplicate field for clang . The reason for duplication is that the clang field will only participate in the shipping configuration, whereas the MSVC variant has been disabled in all. In order to ensure no change of behavior for MSVC toolchain within the clang compilation context, we will keep this in, and deprecate with assignments to the clang setting.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.MSVCUnusedValueWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat values that are computed, but not used (i.e.unnecessary evaluation of an rvalue or expression). Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wunused-value
member.remarks: This is a special case where we have a duplicate field for clang . The reason for duplication is that the clang field will only participate in the shipping configuration, whereas the MSVC variant has been disabled in all. In order to ensure no change of behavior for MSVC toolchain within the clang compilation context, we will keep this in, and deprecate with assignments to the clang setting. -Wunused-result - we will always re-enable this as it's controlled by the same set. This will be moved out to a separate property. It is on by default, so it only must be re-enabled in the disable context.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.MSVCDeprecationWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat usages of functions, methods, or variables, that are marked as deprecated. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wdeprecated-declarations
member.remarks: This is a special case where we have a duplicate field for clang . The reason for duplication is that the clang field will be defaulted to WARNING in clang, but not for msvc which is disabled. In order to ensure no change of behavior for MSVC toolchain within the clang compilation context, we will keep this in, and deprecate with assignments to the clang setting.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.FormatWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat instances of mismatched printf style format specifiers.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.ImplicitIntConversionWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat instances of implicit int conversion. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wimplicit-int-conversion

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.SingleBitfieldConstantConversionWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat instances where a command line argument is provided, but not used for anything.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.UnusedCommandLineArgumentWarningLevel
member.summary: Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wunused-command-line-argument

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.CommentWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat instances of malformed comments. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wcomment

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.RangeLoopConstructWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat instances of copy construct in range loop iteration. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wrange-loop-construct

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.PragmaOnceOutsideHeaderWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat instances where a #pragma once is used in a source file instead of a header. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wpragma-once-outside-header

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.LogicalNotParenthesesWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat instances where the logical NOT operator (!) is used in a way that's ambiguous with respect to parentheses. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wlogical-not-parentheses

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.Cpp20ExtensionsWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat instances where a C++20 feature is being used, but compiling with an older standard. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wc-20-extensions

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.DeprecationWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat usages of functions, methods, or variables, that are marked as deprecated. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wdeprecated-declarations
member.remarks: Disable needed for wxWidgets.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.DeterministicWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat usages of __DATE__ or __TIME__ as they prevent reproducible builds. Clang - https://clang.llvm.org/docs/DiagnosticsReference.html#wdate-time

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.PCHPerformanceIssueWarningLevel
member.summary: Indicates what warning/error level to treat potential PCH performance issues.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileWarnings.ModuleIncludeSubdirectoryWarningLevel

member.name: T:UnrealBuildTool.IWYUSupport
member.summary: To what extent a module supports include-what-you-use

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.IWYUSupport.None
member.summary: None means code does not even compile. IWYU needs to skip this module entirely

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.IWYUSupport.KeepAsIs
member.summary: Module could be modified with iwyu but we want it to stay the way it is and handle changes manually

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.IWYUSupport.KeepAsIsForNow
member.summary: Module is parsed and processed. This means that from the outside it is stripped for includes even though the files are not modified. This can be used to defer iwyu work on a module. When it comes to transitive includes this module is seen as modified from the outside.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.IWYUSupport.KeepPublicAsIsForNow
member.summary: Same as KeepAsIsForNow but will allow iwyu to update private headers and cpp files.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.IWYUSupport.Full
member.summary: Full iwyu support. When running with -Mode=IWYU this module will be modified if needed

member.summary: Explicit private PCH for this module. Implies that this module will not use a shared PCH.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.SharedPCHHeaderFile
member.summary: Header file name for a shared PCH provided by this module. Must be a valid relative path to a public C++ header file. This should only be set for header files that are included by a significant number of other C++ modules.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.ShortName

member.summary: Whether this module should be treated as an engine module (eg. using engine definitions, PCHs, compiled with optimizations enabled in DebugGame configurations, etc...). Initialized to a default based on the rules assembly it was created from.

member.summary: Which engine version's build settings to use by default.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.IncludeOrderVersion
member.summary: What version of include order to use when compiling this module. Can be overridden via -ForceIncludeOrder on the command line or in a module's rules.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.bUseRTTI
member.summary: Use run time type information

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.bVcRemoveUnreferencedComdat
member.summary: Whether to direct MSVC to remove unreferenced COMDAT functions and data.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.bCodeCoverage
member.summary: Enable code coverage compilation/linking support.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.MinCpuArchX64
member.summary: Direct the compiler to generate AVX instructions wherever SSE or AVX intrinsics are used, on the x64 platforms that support it. Note that by enabling this you are changing the minspec for the PC platform, and the resultant executable will crash on machines without AVX support.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.bMinCpuArchAPX
member.summary: Direct the compiler to compile with APX support for X64. Note that by enabling this you are changing the minspec for the PC platform, and the resultant executable will crash on machines without APX support.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.bEnableBufferSecurityChecks
member.summary: Enable buffer security checks. This should usually be enabled as it prevents severe security risks.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.bEnableExceptions
member.summary: Enable exception handling

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.bEnableObjCExceptions
member.summary: Enable objective C exception handling

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.bEnableObjCAutomaticReferenceCounting
member.summary: Enable objective C automatic reference counting (ARC) If you set this to true you should not use shared PCHs for this module. The engine won't be extensively using ARC in the short term Not doing this will result in a compile errors because shared PCHs were compiled with different flags than consumer

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.CppCompileWarningSettings
member.summary: Settings object used by the module.

members.member: Whether to enable all warnings as errors. UE enables most warnings as errors already, but disables a few (such as deprecation warnings). `

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.bDisableStaticAnalysis
member.summary: Disable all static analysis - clang, msvc, pvs-studio.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.bStaticAnalyzerExtensions
member.summary: Enable additional analyzer extension warnings using the EspXEngine plugin. This is only supported for MSVC. See https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cpp/code-quality/using-the-cpp-core-guidelines-checkers This will add a large number of warnings by default. It's recommended to use StaticAnalyzerRulesets if this is enabled.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.StaticAnalyzerRulesets
member.summary: The static analyzer rulesets that should be used to filter warnings. This is only supported for MSVC. See https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cpp/code-quality/using-rule-sets-to-specify-the-cpp-rules-to-run

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.StaticAnalyzerCheckers
member.summary: The static analyzer checkers that should be enabled rather than the defaults. This is only supported for Clang. See https://clang.llvm.org/docs/analyzer/checkers.html for a full list. Or run: 'clang -Xclang -analyzer-checker-help' or: 'clang -Xclang -analyzer-checker-help-alpha' for the list of experimental checkers.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.StaticAnalyzerDisabledCheckers
member.summary: The static analyzer default checkers that should be disabled. Unused if StaticAnalyzerCheckers is populated. This is only supported for Clang. See https://clang.llvm.org/docs/analyzer/checkers.html for a full list. Or run: 'clang -Xclang -analyzer-checker-help' or: 'clang -Xclang -analyzer-checker-help-alpha' for the list of experimental checkers.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.StaticAnalyzerAdditionalCheckers
member.summary: The static analyzer non-default checkers that should be enabled. Unused if StaticAnalyzerCheckers is populated. This is only supported for Clang. See https://clang.llvm.org/docs/analyzer/checkers.html for a full list. Or run: 'clang -Xclang -analyzer-checker-help' or: 'clang -Xclang -analyzer-checker-help-alpha' for the list of experimental checkers.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.StaticAnalyzerPVSDisabledErrors
member.summary: The PVS Studio analysis errors that should be disabled.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.bUseUnity
member.summary: If unity builds are enabled this can be used to override if this specific module will build using Unity. This is set using the per module configurations in BuildConfiguration.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.bMergeUnityFiles
member.summary: Whether to merge module and generated unity files for faster compilation.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.UnityFileTypes
member.summary: Source file types that will be placed into unity files when enabled.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.MinSourceFilesForUnityBuildOverride
member.summary: The number of source files in this module before unity build will be activated for that module. If set to anything besides -1, will override the default setting which is controlled by MinGameModuleSourceFilesForUnityBuild

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.MinFilesUsingPrecompiledHeaderOverride
member.summary: Overrides BuildConfiguration.MinFilesUsingPrecompiledHeader if non-zero.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.NumIncludedBytesPerUnityCPPOverride
member.summary: Overrides Target.NumIncludedBytesPerUnityCPP if non-zero.

member.name: M:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.GetNumIncludedBytesPerUnityCPP
member.summary: Helper function to get the number of byes per unity cpp file

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.bBuildLocallyWithSNDBS
member.summary: Module uses a #import so must be built locally when compiling with SN-DBS

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.IsRedistributableOverride
member.summary: Redistribution override flag for this module.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.bLegalToDistributeObjectCode
member.summary: Whether the output from this module can be publicly distributed, even if it has code/ dependencies on modules that are not (i.e. CarefullyRedist, NotForLicensees, NoRedist). This should be used when you plan to release binaries but not source.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.AllowedRestrictedFolders
member.summary: List of folders which are allowed to be referenced when compiling this binary, without propagating restricted folder names

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.AliasRestrictedFolders
member.summary: Set of aliased restricted folder references

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.IWYUSupport
member.summary: Allows "include what you use" to modify the source code when run. bEnforceIWYU must be true for this variable to matter.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.bAddDefaultIncludePaths
member.summary: Whether to add all the default include paths to the module (eg. the Source/Classes folder, subfolders under Source/Public).

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.bIgnoreUnresolvedSymbols
member.summary: Whether to ignore dangling (i.e. unresolved external) symbols in modules

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.bPrecompile
member.summary: Whether this module should be precompiled. Defaults to the bPrecompile flag from the target. Clear this flag to prevent a module being precompiled.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.bUsePrecompiled
member.summary: Whether this module should use precompiled data. Always true for modules created from installed assemblies.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.bAllowConfidentialPlatformDefines
member.summary: Whether this module can use PLATFORM_XXXX style defines, where XXXX is a confidential platform name. This is used to ensure engine or other shared code does not reveal confidential information inside an #if PLATFORM_XXXX block. Licensee game code may want to allow for them, however.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.bRequiresPlatformSDK
member.summary: Experimental: Whether this module requires platform specific SDK files. Not supported by all platforms yet

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.bDisableAutoRTFMInstrumentation
member.summary: Disables AutoRTFM instrumentation in this module. Only matters when the AutoRTFM compiler is being used. The default value of this depends on whether any `TargetType` can be deduced for the module. It will default to `true` if: - The module has `SupportedTargetTypes` specified and `TargetType.Server` is not one of the target types. - The module is part of a plugin that has a "Type" that is one of "Editor", "EditorAndProgram", "EditorNoCommandlet", or "UncookedOnly". - The module is part of a plugin where the module has `"TargetAllowList"` specified and `"Server"` is not one of the allowed target types. - The module is part of a plugin where the module has `"TargetDenyList"` specified and `"Server"` is one of the denied target types. Otherwise it will default to `false`.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.bInjectAutoRTFMAttributeDisables
member.summary: If `bDisableAutoRTFMInstrumentation` is true, will make the modules `*_API` define contain `AUTORTFM_DISABLE` to let us enable errors for code that uses transactionally-disabled code in places that are transactionally-enabled.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.bAlwaysExportStructs
member.summary: If true, autogenerated functions of USTRUCTS are always exported

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.bAlwaysExportEnums
member.summary: If true, autogenerated functions of UENUMS are always exported

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.bAllowUETypesInNamespaces
member.summary: If true, this module can contain UE types (i.e. UCLASS) in namespaces

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.bMinimizeGeneratedIncludes
member.summary: If true, minimizes the amount of header files included in .generated.h files, which may require more headers from CoreUObject to be explicitly included.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.PublicIncludePathModuleNames
member.summary: List of modules names (no path needed) with header files that our module's public headers needs access to, but we don't need to "import" or link against.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.PublicDependencyModuleNames
member.summary: List of public dependency module names (no path needed) (automatically does the private/public include). These are modules that are required by our public source files.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.PrivateIncludePathModuleNames
member.summary: List of modules name (no path needed) with header files that our module's private code files needs access to, but we don't need to "import" or link against.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.PrivateDependencyModuleNames
member.summary: List of private dependency module names. These are modules that our private code depends on but nothing in our public include files depend on.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.CircularlyReferencedDependentModules
member.summary: Only for legacy reason, should not be used in new code. List of module dependencies that should be treated as circular references. This modules must have already been added to either the public or private dependent module list.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.PublicSystemIncludePaths
member.summary: List of system/library include paths - typically used for External (third party) modules. These are public stable header file directories that are not checked when resolving header dependencies.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.PublicIncludePaths
member.summary: (This setting is currently not need as we discover all files from the 'Public' folder) List of all paths to include files that are exposed to other modules

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.InternalIncludePaths
member.summary: (This setting is currently not need as we discover all files from the 'Internal' folder) List of all paths to include files that are exposed to other internal modules

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.PrivateIncludePaths
member.summary: List of all paths to this module's internal include files, not exposed to other modules (at least one include to the 'Private' path, more if we want to avoid relative paths)

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.PublicSystemLibraryPaths
member.summary: List of system library paths (directory of .lib files) - for External (third party) modules please use the PublicAdditionalLibaries instead

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.PrivateRuntimeLibraryPaths
member.summary: List of search paths for libraries at runtime (eg. .so files)

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.PublicRuntimeLibraryPaths
member.summary: List of search paths for libraries at runtime (eg. .so files)

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.PublicAdditionalLibraries
member.summary: List of additional libraries (names of the .lib files including extension) - typically used for External (third party) modules

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.PublicDebugVisualizerPaths
member.summary: List of additional debug visualizers (.natvis, .natstepfilter, and .natjmc) exposed to other modules - typically used for External (third party) modules

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.DependenciesToSkipPerArchitecture
member.summary: Per-architecture lists of dependencies for linking to ignore (useful when building for multiple architectures, and a lib only is needed for one architecture), it's up to the Toolchain to use this

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.bRequiresImplementModule
member.summary: Whether this module requires the IMPLEMENT_MODULE macro to be implemented. Most UE modules require this, since we use the IMPLEMENT_MODULE macro to do other global overloads (eg. operator new/delete forwarding to GMalloc).

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.ExtraRootPath
member.summary: Module can provide ExtraRootPath. This will be part of the rootpaths table and is needed if module is using paths outside normal environment Id should be the same for all extra roots with the same path otherwise we can end up with cache misses that could be cache hits Make sure path is as long as possible. I.e, don't use "Program Files" as root path but rather "Program Files/MyProgram"

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.VersePath
member.summary: If this module has associated Verse code, this is the Verse root path of it

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.VerseScope
member.summary: Visibility of Verse code in this module's Source/Verse folder

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.bLegacyPublicIncludePaths
member.summary: Whether this module qualifies included headers from other modules relative to the root of their 'Public' folder. This reduces the number of search paths that have to be passed to the compiler, improving performance and reducing the length of the compiler command line.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.bLegacyParentIncludePaths
member.summary: Whether this module qualifies included headers from other modules relative to the parent directory. This reduces the number of search paths that have to be passed to the compiler, improving performance and reducing the length of the compiler command line.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleRules.bValidateCircularDependencies
member.summary: Whether circular dependencies will be validated against the allow list Circular module dependencies result in slower builds. Disabling this option is strongly discouraged. This option is ignored for Engine modules which will always be validated against the allow list.

member.name: T:UnrealBuildTool.TargetType
member.summary: The type of target

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.TargetType.Game
member.summary: Cooked monolithic game executable (GameName.exe). Also used for a game-agnostic engine executable (UnrealGame.exe or RocketGame.exe)

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.TargetType.Editor
member.summary: Uncooked modular editor executable and DLLs (UnrealEditor.exe, UnrealEditor*.dll, GameName*.dll)

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.TargetType.Client
member.summary: Cooked monolithic game client executable (GameNameClient.exe, but no server code)

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.TargetType.Server
member.summary: Cooked monolithic game server executable (GameNameServer.exe, but no client code)

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.TargetType.Program
member.summary: Program (standalone program, e.g. ShaderCompileWorker.exe, can be modular or monolithic depending on the program)

member.name: T:UnrealBuildTool.TargetLinkType
member.summary: Specifies how to link all the modules in this target

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.TargetLinkType.Default
member.summary: Use the default link type based on the current target type

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.TargetLinkType.Monolithic
member.summary: Link all modules into a single binary

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.TargetLinkType.Modular
member.summary: Link modules into individual dynamic libraries

member.name: T:UnrealBuildTool.TargetBuildEnvironment
member.summary: Specifies whether to share engine binaries and intermediates with other projects, or to create project-specific versions. By default, editor builds always use the shared build environment (and engine binaries are written to Engine/Binaries/Platform), but monolithic builds and programs do not (except in installed builds). Using the shared build environment prevents target-specific modifications to the build environment.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.TargetBuildEnvironment.Shared
member.summary: Engine binaries and intermediates are output to the engine folder. Target-specific modifications to the engine build environment will be ignored.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.TargetBuildEnvironment.Unique
member.summary: Engine binaries and intermediates are specific to this target

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.TargetBuildEnvironment.UniqueIfNeeded
member.summary: Will switch to Unique if needed - per-project sdk is enabled, or a property that requires unique is set away from default

member.name: T:UnrealBuildTool.PointerMemberBehavior
member.summary: Specifies how UnrealHeaderTool should enforce member pointer declarations in UCLASSes and USTRUCTs. This should match (by name, not value) the EPointerMemberBehavior enum in BaseParser.h so that it can be interpreted correctly by UnrealHeaderTool.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.PointerMemberBehavior.Disallow
member.summary: Pointer members of the associated type will be disallowed and result in an error emitted from UnrealHeaderTool.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.PointerMemberBehavior.AllowSilently
member.summary: Pointer members of the associated type will be allowed and not emit any messages to log or screen.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.PointerMemberBehavior.AllowAndLog
member.summary: Pointer members of the associated type will be allowed but will emit messages to log.

member.name: T:UnrealBuildTool.BuildSettingsVersion
member.summary: Determines which version of the engine to take default build settings from. This allows for backwards compatibility as new options are enabled by default.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.BuildSettingsVersion.V1
member.summary: Legacy default build settings for 4.23 and earlier.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.BuildSettingsVersion.V2
member.summary: New defaults for 4.24: * ModuleRules.PCHUsage = PCHUsageMode.UseExplicitOrSharedPCHs * ModuleRules.bLegacyPublicIncludePaths = false

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.BuildSettingsVersion.V3
member.summary: New defaults for 5.2: * ModuleRules.bLegacyParentIncludePaths = false

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.BuildSettingsVersion.V4
member.summary: New defaults for 5.3: * TargetRules.CppStandard = CppStandardVersion.Default has changed from Cpp17 to Cpp20 * TargetRules.WindowsPlatform.bStrictConformanceMode = true

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.BuildSettingsVersion.V5
member.summary: New defaults for 5.4: * TargetRules.bValidateFormatStrings = true

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.BuildSettingsVersion.V6
member.summary: New defaults for 5.7: * TargetRules.WindowsPlatform.bStrictInlineConformance = true * ModuleRules.CppCompileWarningSettings.UndefinedIdentifierWarningLevel = WarningLevel.Error

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.BuildSettingsVersion.V7
member.summary: New defaults for 5.8: * ModuleRules.CppCompileWarningSettings.ReturnTypeWarningLevel = WarningLevel.Error * ModuleRules.CppCompileWarningSettings.DanglingWarningLevel = WarningLevel.Error * ModuleRules.CppCompileWarningSettings.UnreachableCodeWarningLevel = WarningLevel.Error

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.BuildSettingsVersion.Latest
member.summary: Always use the defaults for the current engine version. Note that this may cause compatibility issues when upgrading.

member.name: T:UnrealBuildTool.EngineIncludeOrderVersion
member.summary: What version of include order to use when compiling. This controls which UE_ENABLE_INCLUDE_ORDER_DEPRECATED_IN_5_X defines are enabled when compiling the target. The UE_ENABLE_INCLUDE_ORDER_DEPRECATED_IN_5_X defines are used when removing implicit includes from Unreal Public headers. Specific versions can be used by licensees to avoid compile errors when integrating new versions of Unreal. Using Latest comes with a high risk of introducing compile errors in your code on newer Unreal versions.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.EngineIncludeOrderVersion.Unreal5_1
member.summary: Include order used in Unreal 5.1

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.EngineIncludeOrderVersion.Unreal5_2
member.summary: Include order used in Unreal 5.2

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.EngineIncludeOrderVersion.Unreal5_3
member.summary: Include order used in Unreal 5.3

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.EngineIncludeOrderVersion.Unreal5_4
member.summary: Include order used in Unreal 5.4

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.EngineIncludeOrderVersion.Unreal5_5
member.summary: Include order used in Unreal 5.5

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.EngineIncludeOrderVersion.Unreal5_6
member.summary: Include order used in Unreal 5.6

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.EngineIncludeOrderVersion.Unreal5_7
member.summary: Include order used in Unreal 5.7

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.EngineIncludeOrderVersion.Unreal5_8
member.summary: Include order used in Unreal 5.8

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.EngineIncludeOrderVersion.Latest
member.summary: Always use the latest version of include order. This value is updated every Unreal release, use with caution if you intend to integrate newer Unreal releases.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.EngineIncludeOrderVersion.Oldest
member.summary: Contains the oldest version of include order that the engine supports. Do not delete old enum entries to prevent breaking project generation

member.name: T:UnrealBuildTool.StaticAnalyzer
member.summary: Which static analyzer to use

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.StaticAnalyzer.None
member.summary: Do not perform static analysis

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.StaticAnalyzer.Default
member.summary: Use the default static analyzer for the selected compiler, if it has one. For Visual Studio and Clang, this means using their built-in static analysis tools. Any compiler that doesn't support static analysis will ignore this option.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.StaticAnalyzer.VisualCpp
member.summary: Use the built-in Visual C++ static analyzer

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.StaticAnalyzer.PVSStudio
member.summary: Use PVS-Studio for static analysis

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.StaticAnalyzer.Clang
member.summary: Use clang for static analysis. This forces the compiler to clang.

member.name: T:UnrealBuildTool.StaticAnalyzerOutputType
member.summary: Output type for the static analyzer. This currently only works for the Clang static analyzer. The Clang static analyzer can do either Text, which prints the analysis to stdout, or html, where it writes out a navigable HTML page for each issue that it finds, per file. The HTML is output in the same directory as the object file that would otherwise have been generated. All other analyzers default automatically to Text.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.StaticAnalyzerOutputType.Text
member.summary: Output the analysis to stdout.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.StaticAnalyzerOutputType.Html
member.summary: Output the analysis to an HTML file in the object folder.

member.name: T:UnrealBuildTool.StaticAnalyzerMode
member.summary: Output type for the static analyzer. This currently only works for the Clang static analyzer. The Clang static analyzer can do a shallow quick analysis. However the default deep is recommended.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.StaticAnalyzerMode.Deep
member.summary: Default deep analysis.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.StaticAnalyzerMode.Shallow
member.summary: Quick analysis. Not recommended.

member.name: T:UnrealBuildTool.OptimizationMode
member.summary: Optimization mode for compiler settings

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.OptimizationMode.Speed
member.summary: Favor speed

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.OptimizationMode.Size
member.summary: Favor minimal code size

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.OptimizationMode.SizeAndSpeed
member.summary: Somewhere between Speed and Size

member.name: T:UnrealBuildTool.DebugInfoMode
member.summary: Debug info mode for compiler settings to determine how much debug info is available Note that in some cases this flag is used for features which strip certain debug info, in which case the meaning is inverted. i.e. 'Full' removes that type of debug info from all modules

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.DebugInfoMode.None
member.summary: Disable all debugging info. MSVC: object files will be compiled without /Z7 or /Zi but pdbs will still be created callstacks should be available in this mode but there have been reports with them being incorrect

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.DebugInfoMode.Engine
member.summary: Enable debug info for engine modules

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.DebugInfoMode.EnginePlugins
member.summary: Enable debug info for engine plugins

member.name: T:UnrealBuildTool.FPSemanticsMode
member.summary: Floating point math semantics

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.FPSemanticsMode.Default
member.summary: Use the default semantics for the platform.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.FPSemanticsMode.Precise
member.summary: FP math is IEEE-754 compliant, assuming that FP exceptions are disabled and the rounding mode is round-to-nearest-even.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.FPSemanticsMode.Imprecise
member.summary: FP math isn't IEEE-754 compliant: the compiler is allowed to transform math expressions in a ways that might result in differently rounded results from what IEEE-754 requires.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.GameplayDebuggerOverrideState.Full
