member.summary: The current build version

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.Type
member.summary: The type of target.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.DefaultBuildSettings
member.summary: Specifies the engine version to maintain backwards-compatible default build settings with (eg. DefaultSettingsVersion.Release_4_23, DefaultSettingsVersion.Release_4_24). Specify DefaultSettingsVersion.Latest to always use defaults for the current engine version, at the risk of introducing build errors while upgrading.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.ForcedIncludeOrder
member.summary: Force the include order to a specific version. Overrides any Target and Module rules.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.IncludeOrderVersion
member.summary: What version of include order to use when compiling this target. Can be overridden via -ForceIncludeOrder on the command line. ModuleRules.IncludeOrderVersion takes precedence.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.OutputFile
member.summary: Path to the output file for the main executable, relative to the Engine or project directory. This setting is only typically useful for non-UE programs, since the engine uses paths relative to the executable to find other known folders (eg. Content).

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.ConfigValueTracker
member.summary: Tracks a list of config values read while constructing this target

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bUsesCEF3
member.summary: Whether the target uses CEF3.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bUsesSlate
member.summary: Whether the project uses visual Slate UI (as opposed to the low level windowing/messaging, which is always available).

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bUseStaticCRT
member.summary: Forces linking against the static CRT. This is not fully supported across the engine due to the need for allocator implementations to be shared (for example), and TPS libraries to be consistent with each other, but can be used for utility programs.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bDebugBuildsActuallyUseDebugCRT
member.summary: Enables the debug C++ runtime (CRT) for debug builds. By default we always use the release runtime, since the debug version isn't particularly useful when debugging Unreal Engine projects, and linking against the debug CRT libraries forces our third party library dependencies to also be compiled using the debug CRT (and often perform more slowly). Often it can be inconvenient to require a separate copy of the debug versions of third party static libraries simply so that you can debug your program's code.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bLegalToDistributeBinary
member.summary: Whether the output from this target can be publicly distributed, even if it has dependencies on modules that are in folders with special restrictions (eg. CarefullyRedist, NotForLicensees, NoRedist).

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.UndecoratedConfiguration
member.summary: Specifies the configuration whose binaries do not require a "-Platform-Configuration" suffix.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.DecoratedSeparator
member.summary: Specifies the separator charcter for binary filenames.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bAllowHotReload
member.summary: Whether this target supports hot reload

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bBuildAllModules
member.summary: Build all the modules that are valid for this target type. Used for CIS and making installed engine builds.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.VSTestRunSettingsFile
member.summary: Set this to reference a VSTest run settings file from generated projects.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.BuildPlugins
member.summary: Additional plugins that are built for this target type but not enabled.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bRuntimeDependenciesComeFromBuildPlugins
member.summary: If this is true, then the BuildPlugins list will be used to populate RuntimeDependencies, rather than EnablePlugins

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.AdditionalPlugins
member.summary: A list of additional plugins which need to be included in this target. This allows referencing non-optional plugin modules which cannot be disabled, and allows building against specific modules in program targets which do not fit the categories in ModuleHostType.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.EnablePlugins
member.summary: Additional plugins that should be included for this target.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.DisablePlugins
member.summary: List of plugins to be disabled for this target. Note that the project file may still reference them, so they should be marked as optional to avoid failing to find them at runtime.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.OptionalPlugins
member.summary: Additional plugins that should be included for this target if they are found.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bAllowEnginePluginsEnabledByDefault
member.summary: If false, suppress loading "enabled by default" engine plugins not explicitly enabled by the project or target files.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.InternalPluginDependencies
member.summary: A list of Plugin names that are allowed to exist as dependencies without being defined in the uplugin descriptor

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.PakSigningKeysFile
member.summary: Path to the set of pak signing keys to embed in the executable.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.SolutionDirectory
member.summary: Allows a Program Target to specify it's own solution folder path.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bGenerateProgramProject
member.summary: Force a Target to be treated as a Program for the purposes of project file generation.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bExplicitTargetForType
member.summary: If true, GetTargetNameByType will not consider this target, thereby disambiguating -TargetType=X.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bBuildInSolutionByDefault
member.summary: Whether the target should be included in the default solution build configuration Setting this to false will skip building when running in the IDE

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bShouldCompileAsDLL
member.summary: Whether this target should be compiled as a DLL. Requires LinkType to be set to TargetLinkType.Monolithic.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.CustomConfig
member.summary: Extra subdirectory to load config files out of, for making multiple types of builds with the same platform This will get baked into the game executable as CUSTOM_CONFIG and used when staging to filter files and settings

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.ExeBinariesSubFolder

member.summary: Whether to compile the developer tools that are for target platforms or connected devices (defaults to bBuildDeveloperTools)

member.summary: Override for including extra shader formats

member.summary: Enable compilation of UClass and related reflection objects as statically initialized global variables.

member.summary: Enables secure socket connection support for Trace.

member.summary: Enabled for editor builds (TargetType.Editor). Can be overridden for programs (TargetType.Program) that would need to compile against editor code. Not available for other target types. Mainly drives the value of WITH_EDITOR.

member.summary: Whether to compile SpeedTree support.

member.summary: Whether we should compile in support for SpeedTree or not.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bForceEnableExceptions
member.summary: Enable exceptions for all modules.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bUseInlining
member.summary: Enable inlining for all modules.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bForceEnableObjCExceptions
member.summary: Enable exceptions for all modules.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bForceEnableRTTI
member.summary: Enable RTTI for all modules.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bEnablePrivateBuildInformation
member.summary: Enable BuildSettings to contain private information about build. Such as machine name, user name and user domain name (exposed in BuildSettings.h)

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bEnablePIE
member.summary: Enable Position Independent Executable (PIE). Has an overhead cost

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bEnableStackProtection
member.summary: Enable Stack Protection. Has an overhead cost

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bWithClientCode
member.summary: Compile client-only code.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bWithServerCode
member.summary: Compile server-only code.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bFNameOutlineNumber
member.summary: Compile with FName storing the number part in the name table. Saves memory when most names are not numbered and those that are are referenced multiple times. The game and engine must ensure they reuse numbered names similarly to name strings to avoid leaking memory.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bWithPushModel
member.summary: When enabled, Push Model Networking support will be compiled in. This can help reduce CPU overhead of networking, at the cost of more memory. Always enabled in editor builds.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bExcludeParallelIrisSupport
member.summary: When enabled, certain thread safety mechanisms will be removed from Iris code this may result in a small cpu perf gain if running a single-threaded server

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bCompileWithStatsWithoutEngine
member.summary: Whether to include stats support even without the engine.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bCompileWithPluginSupport
member.summary: Whether to include plugin support.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bIncludePluginsForTargetPlatforms
member.summary: Whether to allow plugins which support all target platforms.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bCompileWithAccessibilitySupport
member.summary: Whether to allow accessibility code in both Slate and the OS layer.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bWithPerfCounters
member.summary: Whether to include PerfCounters support.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bWithLiveCoding
member.summary: Whether to enable support for live coding

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bUseDebugLiveCodingConsole
member.summary: Whether to enable support for live coding

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bWithFixedTimeStepSupport
member.summary: Whether to enable the support for FixedTimeStep in the engine

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bUseLoggingInShipping
member.summary: Whether to turn on logging for test/shipping builds.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bUseConsoleInShipping
member.summary: Whether to turn on console for shipping builds.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bLoggingToMemoryEnabled
member.summary: Whether to turn on logging to memory for test/shipping builds.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bUseLauncherChecks
member.summary: Whether to check that the process was launched through an external launcher.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bUseChecksInShipping
member.summary: Whether to turn on checks (asserts) for test/shipping builds.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bAllowProfileGPUInTest
member.summary: Whether to turn on GPU markers for Test builds.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bAllowProfileGPUInShipping
member.summary: Whether to turn on GPU markers for Shipping builds.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bTCHARIsUTF8
member.summary: Whether to turn on UTF-8 mode, mapping TCHAR to UTF8CHAR.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bUseEstimatedUtcNow
member.summary: Whether to use the EstimatedUtcNow or PlatformUtcNow. EstimatedUtcNow is appropriate in cases where PlatformUtcNow can be slow.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bCompileFreeType
member.summary: True if we need FreeType support.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bUseExecCommandsInShipping
member.summary: Whether to turn allow exec commands for shipping builds.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.OptimizationLevel
member.summary: Allows to fine tune optimizations level for speed and\or code size

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bDefineForceInlineHintToInline
member.summary: By default UE_FORCEINLINE_HINT is defined to FORCEINLINE. If this flag is true it will instead be defined to 'inline'. This flag should only be true for targets where code size is a concern and/or is being compiled with PGO or LTO. Note that if this flag is true when PGOOptimize is enabled, it should also be true when PGOProfile is enabled.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bUseSparseSetStrictMode
member.summary: If enabled sparse set will have additional runtime checks against usage that conflicts with compact sets. This can be used to catch

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bUseCompactSetAsDefault
member.summary: By default TSparseSet is used as the underlying TSet container. Use this flag to change that to use TCompactSet instead.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bUseAndroidJni
member.summary: Whether to use Android Java Native Interface.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.FPSemantics
member.summary: Allows setting the FP semantics.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bForceCompileDevelopmentAutomationTests
member.summary: Whether to compile development automation tests.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bForceCompilePerformanceAutomationTests
member.summary: Whether to compile performance automation tests.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bForceDisableAutomationTests
member.summary: Whether to override the defaults for automation tests (Debug/Development configs)

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bEventDrivenLoader
member.summary: If true, event driven loader will be used in cooked builds. @todoio This needs to be replaced by a runtime solution after async loading refactor.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.NativePointerMemberBehaviorOverride
member.summary: Used to override the behavior controlling whether UCLASSes and USTRUCTs are allowed to have native pointer members, if disallowed they will be a UHT error and should be substituted with TObjectPtr members instead.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bUseXGEController
member.summary: Whether the XGE controller worker and modules should be included in the engine build. These are required for distributed shader compilation using the XGE interception interface.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bIWYU
member.summary: Enables "include what you use" by default for modules in this target. Changes the default PCH mode for any module in this project to PCHUsageMode.UseExplicitOrSharedPCHs.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bIncludeHeaders
member.summary: Adds header files in included modules to the build.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bHeadersOnly
member.summary: When used with -IncludeHeaders, only header files will be compiled.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bEnforceIWYU
member.summary: Enforce "include what you use" rules; warns if monolithic headers (Engine.h, UnrealEd.h, etc...) are used, and checks that source files include their matching header first.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bWarnAboutMonolithicHeadersIncluded
member.summary: Emit a warning when an old-style monolithic header is included while compiling this target.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bHasExports
member.summary: Whether the final executable should export symbols.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bPrecompile
member.summary: Make static libraries for all engine modules as intermediates for this target.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bEnableOSX109Support
member.summary: Whether we should compile with support for OS X 10.9 Mavericks. Used for some tools that we need to be compatible with this version of OS X.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bIsBuildingConsoleApplication
member.summary: True if this is a console application that's being built.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bBuildAdditionalConsoleApp
member.summary: If true, creates an additional console application. Hack for Windows, where it's not possible to conditionally inherit a parent's console Window depending on how the application is invoked; you have to link the same executable with a different subsystem setting.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bBuildConsoleAppOnly
member.summary: If true, only creates an additional console application. Overrides bBuildAdditionalConsoleApp.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bDisableSymbolCache
member.summary: True if debug symbols that are cached for some platforms should not be created.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bUseUnityBuild
member.summary: Whether to unify C++ code into larger files for faster compilation.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bUseUnityBuildOverride
member.summary: Whether to unify C++ code into larger files for faster compilation.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bForceUnityBuild
member.summary: Whether to force C++ source files to be combined into larger files for faster compilation.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bAlwaysUseUnityForGeneratedFiles
member.summary: Whether to force generated files to be placed in a unity file even when unity files are otherwise disabled. It is not recommended to disable this unless debugging issues with UHT file generation.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.DisableMergingModuleAndGeneratedFilesInUnityFiles
member.summary: List of modules that disables merging module and generated cpp files in the same unity files.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bUseAdaptiveUnityBuild
member.summary: Use a heuristic to determine which files are currently being iterated on and exclude them from unity blobs. This results in faster incremental compile times. For Perforce repositories, the current implementation uses the read-only flag to distinguish the working set, assuming that files will be made writable by the source control system if they are being modified. For Git repositories, the implementation uses the git status command. Source code archives downloaded from Git as .zip files are not supported.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bAdaptiveUnityDisablesOptimizations
member.summary: Disable optimization for files that are in the adaptive non-unity working set.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.AdaptiveUnityDisablesOptimizationsConfigurations
member.summary: Disable optimizations for files that are in the adaptive non-unity working set for the given configurations.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bAdaptiveUnityDisablesPCH
member.summary: Disables force-included PCHs for files that are in the adaptive non-unity working set.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bAdaptiveUnityDisablesProjectPCHForProjectPrivate
member.summary: Backing storage for bAdaptiveUnityDisablesProjectPCH.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bAdaptiveUnityDisablesPCHForProject
member.summary: Whether to disable force-included PCHs for project source files in the adaptive non-unity working set. Defaults to bAdaptiveUnityDisablesPCH;

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bAdaptiveUnityCreatesDedicatedPCH
member.summary: Creates a dedicated PCH for each source file in the working set, allowing faster iteration on cpp-only changes.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bAdaptiveUnityEnablesEditAndContinue
member.summary: Creates a dedicated PCH for each source file in the working set, allowing faster iteration on cpp-only changes.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bAdaptiveUnityCompilesHeaderFiles
member.summary: Creates a dedicated source file for each header file in the working set to detect missing includes in headers.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.MinGameModuleSourceFilesForUnityBuild
member.summary: The number of source files in a game module before unity build will be activated for that module. This allows small game modules to have faster iterative compile times for single files, at the expense of slower full rebuild times. This setting can be overridden by the bFasterWithoutUnity option in a module's Build.cs file.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.DefaultWarningLevel
member.summary: Default treatment of uncategorized warnings

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bRequireObjectPtrForAddReferencedObjects
member.summary: Require TObjectPtr for FReferenceCollector API's. (Needed for compatibility with incremental GC.)

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bValidateFormatStrings
member.summary: Emits compilation errors for incorrect UE_LOG format strings.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.CppCompileWarningSettings
member.summary: C++ Warnings settings object used by the target

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bWarningsAsErrors
member.summary: Whether to enable all warnings as errors. UE enables most warnings as errors already, but disables a few (such as deprecation warnings).

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bShowIncludes
member.summary: Print out files that are included by each source file

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bRetainFramePointers
member.summary: Forces frame pointers to be retained this is usually required when you want reliable callstacks e.g. mallocframeprofiler

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bRetainFramePointersOverride
member.summary: Forces frame pointers to be retained this is usually required when you want reliable callstacks e.g. mallocframeprofiler

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bUseFastMonoCalls
member.summary: New Monolithic Graphics drivers have optional "fast calls" replacing various D3d functions

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.NumIncludedBytesPerUnityCPP
member.summary: An approximate number of bytes of C++ code to target for inclusion in a single unified C++ file.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bDisableModuleNumIncludedBytesPerUnityCPPOverride
member.summary: Disables overrides that are set by the module

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bStressTestUnity
member.summary: Whether to stress test the C++ unity build robustness by including all C++ files files in a project from a single unified file.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bForceDebugInfo
member.summary: Whether to force debug info to be generated.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.DebugInfo
member.summary: How much debug info should be generated. See DebugInfoMode enum for more details

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.DisableDebugInfoModules
member.summary: Modules that should have debug info disabled

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.DisableDebugInfoPlugins
member.summary: Plugins that should have debug info disabled

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.DebugInfoLineTablesOnly
member.summary: True if only debug line number tables should be emitted in debug information for compilers that support doing so. Overrides TargetRules.DebugInfo See https://clang.llvm.org/docs/UsersManual.html#cmdoption-gline-tables-only for more information

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.DebugInfoLineTablesOnlyModules
member.summary: Modules that should emit line number tables instead of full debug information for compilers that support doing so. Overrides DisableDebugInfoModules

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.DebugInfoLineTablesOnlyPlugins
member.summary: Plugins that should emit line number tables instead of full debug information for compilers that support doing so. Overrides DisableDebugInfoPlugins

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.DebugInfoNoInlineLineTables
member.summary: True if debug line number tables should be omitted in debug information for inline functions, compilers that support doing so.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.DebugInfoInlineLineTablesModules
member.summary: Modules that should include line number tables for inline functions for compilers that support doing so, even if DebugInfoNoInlineLineTables would otherwise remove them.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.DebugInfoInlineLineTablesPlugins
member.summary: Modules that should include line number tables for inline functions for compilers that support doing so, even if DebugInfoNoInlineLineTables would otherwise remove them.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.DebugInfoSimpleTemplateNames
member.summary: True if template type names should be simplified to reduce the size of debug info for compilers that support doing so.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.DebugInfoSimpleTemplateNamesModules
member.summary: Modules for which template type names should be simplified to reduce the size of debug info for compilers that support doing so.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.DebugInfoSimpleTemplateNamesPlugins
member.summary: Plugins for which template type names should be simplified to reduce the size of debug info for compilers that support doing so.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bDisableDebugInfoForGeneratedCode
member.summary: Whether to disable debug info generation for generated files. This improves link times and reduces pdb size for modules that have a lot of generated glue code.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bOmitPCDebugInfoInDevelopment
member.summary: Whether to disable debug info on PC/Mac in development builds (for faster developer iteration, as link times are extremely fast with debug info disabled).

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bUsePDBFiles
member.summary: Whether PDB files should be used for Visual C++ builds.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bUsePCHFiles
member.summary: Whether PCH files should be used.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bDeterministic
member.summary: Set flags require for deterministic compiling and linking. Enabling deterministic mode for msvc disables codegen multithreading so compiling will be slower

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bUseVFS
member.summary: Experimental work in progress: Set flags to use toolchain virtual file system support, to generate consistent pathing in outputs

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.CacheSalt
member.summary: Salt value to modify cache bucket calculation, can be used to invalidate cached actions outputs

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bChainPCHs
member.summary: Whether PCHs should be chained when compiling with clang. Note: The earliest PCH a header is included in the chain will be included, so if there is a mismatch in preprocessor state this can cause an unexpected version of a header to be included. Currently disabled as this is the case.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bForceIncludePCHHeadersForGenCppFilesWhenPCHIsDisabled
member.summary: Whether PCH headers should be force included for gen.cpp files when PCH is disabled.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bPreprocessOnly
member.summary: Whether to just preprocess source files for this target, and skip compilation

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bWithAssembly
member.summary: Whether to generate assembly data while compiling this target. Works exclusively on MSVC for now.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bAllowUbaCompression
member.summary: Experimental: Store object (.obj) compressed on disk. Requires UBA to link. Toggling this flag will invalidate actions.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.StaticAnalyzer
member.summary: Whether static code analysis should be enabled.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.StaticAnalyzerOutputType
member.summary: The output type to use for the static analyzer. This is only supported for Clang.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.StaticAnalyzerMode
member.summary: The mode to use for the static analyzer. This is only supported for Clang. Shallow mode completes quicker but is generally not recommended.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.StaticAnalyzerPVSPrintLevel
member.summary: The level of warnings to print when analyzing using PVS-Studio

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.StaticAnalyzerPVSConfigs
member.summary: Paths to PVS-Studio .pvsconfig configuration files

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bStaticAnalyzerProjectOnly
member.summary: Only run static analysis against project modules, skipping engine modules
member.remarks: PVS-Studio: warnings that are disabled via Engine/Source/Runtime/Core/Public/Microsoft/MicrosoftPlatformCodeAnalysis.h will be reenabled

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bStaticAnalyzerIncludeGenerated
member.summary: When enabled, generated source files will be analyzed

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.MinFilesUsingPrecompiledHeader
member.summary: The minimum number of files that must use a pre-compiled header before it will be created and used.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bForcePrecompiledHeaderForGameModules
member.summary: When enabled, a precompiled header is always generated for game modules, even if there are only a few source files in the module. This greatly improves compile times for iterative changes on a few files in the project, at the expense of slower full rebuild times for small game projects. This can be overridden by setting MinFilesUsingPrecompiledHeaderOverride in a module's Build.cs file.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bUseIncrementalLinking
member.summary: Whether to use incremental linking or not. Incremental linking can yield faster iteration times when making small changes. Currently disabled by default because it tends to behave a bit buggy on some computers (PDB-related compile errors).

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bAllowLTCG
member.summary: Whether to allow the use of link time code generation (LTCG).

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bPreferThinLTO
member.summary: When Link Time Code Generation (LTCG) is enabled, whether to prefer using the lighter weight version on supported platforms.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.ThinLTOCacheDirectory
member.summary: Directory where to put the ThinLTO cache on supported platforms.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.ThinLTOCachePruningArguments
member.summary: Arguments that will be applied to prune the ThinLTO cache on supported platforms. Arguments will only be applied if ThinLTOCacheDirectory is set.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bThinLTODistributed
member.summary: When ThinLTO is enabled we use distributed linking if supported This can save a lot of time for large binaries

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bPGOProfile
member.summary: Whether to enable Profile Guided Optimization (PGO) instrumentation in this build.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bPGOOptimize
member.summary: Whether to optimize this build with Profile Guided Optimization (PGO).

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bCodeCoverage
member.summary: Whether the target requires code coverage compilation and linking.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bSupportEditAndContinue
member.summary: Whether to support edit and continue.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bOmitFramePointers
member.summary: Whether to omit frame pointers or not. Disabling is useful for e.g. memory profiling on the PC.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bCompressDebugFile
member.summary: If we want to compress the debug files

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bEnableCppModules
member.summary: Whether to enable support for C++20 modules

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bEnableProcessPriorityControl
member.summary: Whether to enable engine's ability to set process priority on runtime. This option requires some environment setup on Linux, that's why it's disabled by default. Project has to opt-in this feature as it has to guarantee correct setup.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bShaderCompilerWorkerTrace
member.summary: If true, then enable Unreal Insights (utrace) profiling in the build for the Shader Compiler Worker (defines USE_SHADER_COMPILER_WORKER_TRACE=1).

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bUseSharedPCHs
member.summary: Enables "Shared PCHs", a feature which significantly speeds up compile times by attempting to share certain PCH files between modules that UBT detects is including those PCH's header files.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bUseShippingPhysXLibraries
member.summary: True if Development and Release builds should use the release configuration of PhysX/APEX.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bUseCheckedPhysXLibraries
member.summary: True if Development and Release builds should use the checked configuration of PhysX/APEX. if bUseShippingPhysXLibraries is true this is ignored.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bCheckLicenseViolations
member.summary: Tells the UBT to check if module currently being built is violating EULA.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bBreakBuildOnLicenseViolation
member.summary: Tells the UBT to break build if module currently being built is violating EULA.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bCreateMapFile
member.summary: Outputs a map file as part of the build.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bAllowRuntimeSymbolFiles
member.summary: True if runtime symbols files should be generated as a post build step for some platforms. These files are used by the engine to resolve symbol names of callstack backtraces in logs.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.PackagePath
member.summary: Package full path (directory + filename) where to store input files used at link time Normally used to debug a linker crash for platforms that support it

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.CrashDiagnosticDirectory
member.summary: Directory where to put crash report files for platforms that support it

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.BundleVersion
member.summary: Bundle version for Mac apps.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bDeployAfterCompile
member.summary: Whether to deploy the executable after compilation on platforms that require deployment.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bForceSkipDeploy
member.summary: Whether to force skipping deployment for platforms that require deployment by default.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bAllowRemotelyCompiledPCHs
member.summary: When enabled, allows XGE to compile pre-compiled header files on remote machines. Otherwise, PCHs are always generated locally.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bUseHeaderUnitsForPch
member.summary: Will replace pch with ifc and use header units instead. This is an experimental and msvc-only feature

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bCheckSystemHeadersForModification
member.summary: Whether headers in system paths should be checked for modification when determining outdated actions.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bDisableLinking
member.summary: Whether to disable linking for this target.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bIgnoreBuildOutputs
member.summary: Whether to ignore tracking build outputs for this target.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bDisableEngineDeprecations
member.summary: Whether to disable UE_DEPRECATED_FORENGINE deprecation warnings in non-engine modules. This should only be used in the short-term as these deprecations will get removed.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bFormalBuild
member.summary: Indicates that this is a formal build, intended for distribution. This flag is automatically set to true when Build.version has a changelist set and is a promoted build. The only behavior currently bound to this flag is to compile the default resource file separately for each binary so that the OriginalFilename field is set correctly. By default, we only compile the resource once to reduce build times.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bFlushBuildDirOnRemoteMac
member.summary: Whether to clean Builds directory on a remote Mac before building.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bPrintToolChainTimingInfo
member.summary: Whether to write detailed timing info from the compiler and linker.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bParseTimingInfoForTracing
member.summary: Whether to parse timing data into a tracing file compatible with chrome://tracing.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bPublicSymbolsByDefault
member.summary: Whether to expose all symbols as public by default on POSIX platforms

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bIdenticalCodeFolding
member.summary: Enables Identical Code Folding (Clang toolchains)

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.ToolChainName
member.summary: Allows overriding the toolchain to be created for this target. This must match the name of a class declared in the UnrealBuildTool assembly.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.MSVCCompileActionWeight
member.summary: The weight(cpu/memory utilization) of a MSVC compile action

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.ClangCompileActionWeight
member.summary: The weight(cpu/memory utilization) of a clang compile action

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bDisableUnverifiedCertificates
member.summary: Whether to allow engine configuration to determine if we can load unverified certificates.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bAllowGeneratedIniWhenCooked
member.summary: Whether to load generated ini files in cooked build, (GameUserSettings.ini loaded either way)

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bAllowNonUFSIniWhenCooked
member.summary: Whether to load non-ufs ini files in cooked build, (GameUserSettings.ini loaded either way)

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bLegacyPublicIncludePaths
member.summary: Add all the public folders as include paths for the compile environment.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bLegacyParentIncludePaths
member.summary: Add all the parent folders as include paths for the compile environment.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.CppStandardEngine
member.summary: Which C++ standard to use for compiling this target (for engine modules)

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.CppStandard
member.summary: Which C++ standard to use for compiling this target (for non-engine modules)

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.CStandard
member.summary: Which C standard to use for compiling this target

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.MinCpuArchX64
member.summary: Direct the compiler to generate AVX instructions wherever SSE or AVX intrinsics are used, on the x64 platforms that support it. Ignored for arm64. Note that by enabling this you are changing the minspec for the target platform, and the resultant executable will crash on machines without AVX support.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bMinCpuArchAPX
member.summary: Direct the compiler to compile with APX support for X64. Note that by enabling this you are changing the minspec for the PC platform, and the resultant executable will crash on machines without APX support.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.MinArm64CpuTarget
member.summary: Select a minimum ARM64 target CPU. This will change what ARMv8(9)-A ISA + extension code will be compiled for. Changing it from default will guarantee not to work on all ARM64 CPUs. Currently supported by clang compiler only

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bNoManifestChanges
member.summary: Do not allow manifest changes when building this target. Used to cause earlier errors when building multiple targets with a shared build environment.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.BuildVersion
member.summary: The build version string

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.LinkType
member.summary: Specifies how to link modules in this target (monolithic or modular). This is currently protected for backwards compatibility. Call the GetLinkType() accessor until support for the deprecated ShouldCompileMonolithic() override has been removed.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bStripExports
member.summary: Experimental: Strip unused exports from libraries. Only applies when LinkType is Modular

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bMergeModules
member.summary: Experimental: Merge modular modules into combined libraries. Sets LinkType to Modular and enables bStripExports

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bCheckDelegateLifetime
member.summary: Experimental: Check lifetimes for delegate instances. Recommended for merged modules testing

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.MergeAdditionalCommonModules
member.summary: Experimental: List of modules to exclude from Engine to be placed in Common

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.MergePlugins
member.summary: Experimental: List of plugins (and their dependencies) to each merge into separate libraries. Requires bMergeModules to be enabled

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.MergePluginsIgnored
member.summary: Experimental: List of plugins (and their dependencies) to ignore when processing merged libraries. Requires bMergeModules to be enabled Ignored plugins will still be compiled, but if they're kept in the base executable, their dependencies will not be forced out of merged library with them. This should be used carefully, for organisational plugins known to have dependencies they don't actually need at runtime.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.MergePluginsExcluded
member.summary: Experimental: List of plugins (and their dependencies) to explicitly exclude when processing merged libraries. Requires bMergeModules to be enabled Ignored plugins will still be compiled but forced into the executable.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.MergePluginsShared
member.summary: Experimental: List of merged plugins to move common dependencies to a shared library, requires MergePlugins. Can be chained

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.MergePluginsLaunch
member.summary: Experimental: List of merged plugins to move to the main executable, requires MergePlugins. "Engine" for all shared engine modules and "Common" for shared project modules.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bMergeOnlyGameFeatures
member.summary: Experimental: Whether to merge only game feature plugins, ignoring regular plugins.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bReportModuleMergingData
member.summary: Experimental: Whether to report module merging data to the game target.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.LinkTypePrivate
member.summary: Backing storage for the LinkType property.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.GlobalDefinitions
member.summary: Macros to define globally across the whole target.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.ProjectDefinitions
member.summary: Macros to define across all macros in the project.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.BuildSettingsVariables
member.summary: Variables that will be stored in BuildSettings module. Can be fetched with BuildSettings::GetVariable

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.AdditionalProperties
member.summary: Environment variables. Should be used instead of Environment.Set/GetEnvironmentVariable in .build.cs

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.LaunchModuleName
member.summary: Specifies the name of the launch module. For modular builds, this is the module that is compiled into the target's executable.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.LaunchModuleNamePrivate
member.summary: Backing storage for the LaunchModuleName property.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.ExportPublicHeader
member.summary: Specifies the path to write a header containing public definitions for this target. Useful when building a DLL to be consumed by external build processes.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.ExtraModuleNames
member.summary: List of additional modules to be compiled into the target.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.ManifestFileNames
member.summary: Path to a manifest to output for this target

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.DependencyListFileNames
member.summary: Path to a list of dependencies for this target, when precompiling

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.BuildEnvironmentOverride
member.summary: Backing storage for the BuildEnvironment property

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.BuildEnvironment
member.summary: Specifies the build environment for this target. See TargetBuildEnvironment for more information on the available options.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bAllowSDKOverrideModulesWithSharedEnvironment
member.summary: If this true, a Shared build environment target will allow for any modules that are SDK version sensitive to have a project side module when an SDK is overridden. For instance, if IOSTargetPlatform, which is marked as IOS SDK version relevant, is compiled for a Target with this set to true, and that target overrides the IOS SDK, then this would compile to e.g. MyProject/Binaries/Win64/IOS/MyProject-IOSTargetPlatform.dll

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.bOverrideBuildEnvironment
member.summary: Whether to ignore violations to the shared build environment (eg. editor targets modifying definitions)

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.PreBuildTargets
member.summary: Specifies a list of targets which should be built before this target is built.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.PreBuildSteps
member.summary: Specifies a list of steps which should be executed before this target is built, in the context of the host platform's shell. The following variables will be expanded before execution: $(EngineDir), $(ProjectDir), $(TargetName), $(TargetPlatform), $(TargetConfiguration), $(TargetType), $(ProjectFile).

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules.PostBuildSteps

member.summary: Indicates whether this platform requires a .loadorder file to be generated for the build. .loadorder files contain a list of dynamic modules and the exact order in which they should be loaded to ensure that all dependencies are satisfied i.e. we don't attempt to load a module without loading all its dependencies first. As such, this file is only needed in modular builds on some platforms (depending on the way they implement dynamic modules such as DLLs).
member.returns: True if .loadorder file should be generated

member.name: M:UnrealBuildTool.UEBuildTarget.CleanStaleModules(Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger)
member.summary:
Cleans any stale modules that have changed output folder.
On Windows, the loader reads imported DLLs from the first location it finds them. If modules are moved from one place to another, we have to be sure to clean up the old versions so that they're not loaded accidentally causing unintuitive import errors.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.UnrealBuildAcceleratorConfig.bDisableRemote
member.summary: When set to true, UBA will not use any remote help

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.UnrealBuildAcceleratorConfig.bForceBuildAllRemote
member.summary: When set to true, UBA will force all actions that can be built remotely to be built remotely. This will hang if there are no remote agents available

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.UnrealBuildAcceleratorConfig.bAllowRetry
member.summary: When set to true, actions that fail locally with certain error codes will retry without uba.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.UnrealBuildAcceleratorConfig.bForcedRetry
member.summary: When set to true, all actions that fail locally with UBA will be retried without UBA.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.UnrealBuildAcceleratorConfig.bForcedRetryRemote
member.summary: When set to true, actions that fail remotely with UBA will be retried locally with UBA.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.UnrealBuildAcceleratorConfig.bStrict
member.summary: When set to true, all errors and warnings from UBA will be output at the appropriate severity level to the log (rather than being output as 'information' and attempting to continue regardless).

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.UnrealBuildAcceleratorConfig.bStoreRaw
member.summary: If UBA should store cas compressed or raw

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.UnrealBuildAcceleratorConfig.bLinkRemote
member.summary: If UBA should distribute linking to remote workers. This needs bandwidth but can be an optimization

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.UnrealBuildAcceleratorConfig.StoreCapacityGb

member.summary: Max number of worker threads that can handle messages from remotes.

member.summary: Max size of each message sent from server to client

member.summary: Which ip UBA server should listen to for connections

member.summary: Which port UBA server should listen to for connections.

member.summary: Use Quic protocol instead of Tcp (experimental)

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.UnrealBuildAcceleratorConfig.bWriteToDisk
member.summary: Skip writing intermediate and output files to disk. Useful for validation builds where we don't need the output

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.UnrealBuildAcceleratorConfig.bDisableCustomAlloc
member.summary: Set to true to disable mimalloc and detouring of memory allocations.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.UnrealBuildAcceleratorConfig.Zone
member.summary: The zone to use for UBA.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.UnrealBuildAcceleratorConfig.bUseCrypto
member.summary: Set to true to enable encryption when transferring files over the network.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.UnrealBuildAcceleratorConfig.bUseKnownInputs
member.summary: Set to true to provide known inputs to processes that are run remote. This is an experimental feature to speed up build times when ping is higher

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.UnrealBuildAcceleratorConfig.ActionsOutputFile
member.summary: Write yaml file with all actions that are queued for build. This can be used to replay using "UbaCli.exe local file.yaml"

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.UnrealBuildAcceleratorConfig.bDetailedLog
member.summary: Set to true to see more info about what is happening inside uba and also log output from agents

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.UnrealBuildAcceleratorConfig.bAllowDetour
member.summary: Disable all detouring making all actions run outside uba

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.UnrealBuildAcceleratorConfig.bWritePlaceholders
member.summary: Write zero-byte placeholder files for exe/dll/pdb instead of the full files. This can be useful for validation builds or builds just populating cache

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.UnrealBuildAcceleratorConfig.SharedMemoryTempFile
member.summary: Makes the shared memory used in UBA to be backed by a Temp+DeleteOnClose file. This trades committed memory charge for the risk of more I/O under RAM pressure

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.UnrealBuildAcceleratorConfig.MaxRacingPercent
member.summary: With this enabled local machine will start local processes to race remotes when utilization goes under percent number So if local machine can run 20 actions and only run 10, that means that it is 50% utilized. If MaxRacingPercent is set to 70%, local machine will look at what actions remotes are running and start running them too until it reaches 70% utilization

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.UnrealBuildAcceleratorConfig.CacheMaxWorkers
member.summary: Max number of cache download tasks that can execute in parallel

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.UnrealBuildAcceleratorConfig.bCacheShuffle
member.summary: Shuffle the list of cache servers before each read attempt

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.UnrealBuildAcceleratorConfig.bForceNoCache
member.summary: Shuffle the list of cache servers before each read attempt

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.UnrealBuildAcceleratorConfig.bReportCacheMissReason
member.summary: Report reason a cache miss happened. Useful when searching for determinism/portability issues

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.UnrealBuildAcceleratorConfig.bCacheLinkActions
member.summary: Control if link actions should be downloaded from cache or not

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.UnrealBuildAcceleratorConfig.CompressionLevel
member.summary: Compression level. Options are None, SuperFast, VeryFast, Fast, Normal, Optimal1 to Optimal5

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.UnrealBuildAcceleratorConfig.ProcessorCountMultiplier
member.summary: Processor count multiplier for local execution. Can be below 1 to reserve CPU for other tasks. When using the local executor (not XGE), run a single action on each CPU core. Note that you can set this to a larger value to get slightly faster build times in many cases, but your computer's responsiveness during compiling may be much worse. This value is ignored if the CPU does not support hyper-threading.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.UnrealBuildAcceleratorConfig.MemoryPerActionBytes
member.summary: Free memory per action in bytes, used to limit the number of parallel actions if the machine is memory starved. Set to 0 to disable free memory checking.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.UnrealBuildAcceleratorConfig.ProcessPriority
member.summary: The priority to set for spawned processes. Valid Settings: Idle, BelowNormal, Normal, AboveNormal, High Default: BelowNormal or Normal for an Asymmetrical processor as BelowNormal can cause scheduling issues.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.UnrealBuildAcceleratorConfig.bStopCompilationAfterErrors
member.summary: When enabled, will stop compiling targets after a compile error occurs.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.UnrealBuildAcceleratorConfig.StopCompilationAfterNumErrors
member.summary: When set, will stop compiling targets after a set number of errors occurs.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.UnrealBuildAcceleratorConfig.bShowCompilationTimes
member.summary: Whether to show compilation times along with worst offenders or not.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.UnrealBuildAcceleratorConfig.bShowPerActionCompilationTimes
member.summary: Whether to show compilation times for each executed action

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.UnrealBuildAcceleratorConfig.bLogActionCommandLines
member.summary: Whether to log command lines for actions being executed

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.UnrealBuildAcceleratorConfig.bPrintActionTargetNames
member.summary: Add target names for each action executed

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.UnrealBuildAcceleratorConfig.bShowCPUUtilization
member.summary: Whether to show CPU utilization after the work is complete.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.UnrealBuildAcceleratorConfig.bCleanupOnDispose
member.summary: Whether to cleanup the UBA executor's resources on dispose. This will result in resources being freed sooner, rather than waiting until process shutdown, at the expense of taking longer before the UBA process terminates. Unless you know your UBT process will significantly outlive the UBA executor and need to free up resources now, it's suggested you leave this as false.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.UnrealBuildAcceleratorConfig.CanRetryNatively
member.summary: Whether UBA can retry actions natively if they fail when detoured locally

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.UnrealBuildAcceleratorConfig.UseAgentStore
member.summary: Disable this to stop UbaAgent instances to use casdb store

member.summary: Full path of file using forward paths, eg: d:/dev/folder/file.h

member.summary: Transitive includes. This is all the includes that someone gets for free when including this file Note, this is based on what iwyu believes should be in all includes. So it will not look at "seen includes" and only use its generated list.

member.summary: Which directory to run IWYU on. Note this can be combined with -ModuleToUpdate to double filter PathToUpdate supports multiple paths using semi colon separation

member.summary: Same as PathToUpdate but provide the list of paths in a text file instead. Add all the desired paths on a separate line

member.summary: Will remove headers that are needed but redundant because they are included through other needed includes

member.name: M:UnrealBuildTool.ApplePlatformSDK.VersionToIntRegex
member.remarks:
Pattern:
^(\\d+).(\\d+)(.(\\d+))?(.(\\d+))?(.(\\d+))?$
Explanation:
○ Match if at the beginning of the string.
○ 1st capture group.
○ Match a Unicode digit greedily at least once.
○ Match any character other than '\n'.
○ 2nd capture group.
○ Match a Unicode digit greedily at least once.
○ Optional (greedy).
○ 3rd capture group.
○ Match any character other than '\n'.
○ 4th capture group.
○ Match a Unicode digit greedily at least once.
○ Optional (greedy).
○ 5th capture group.
○ Match any character other than '\n'.
○ 6th capture group.
○ Match a Unicode digit greedily at least once.
○ Optional (greedy).
○ 7th capture group.
○ Match any character other than '\n'.
○ 8th capture group.
○ Match a Unicode digit atomically at least once.
○ Match if at the end of the string or if before an ending newline.

member.name: M:UnrealBuildTool.ClangToolChain.ClangToolChainInfo.ClangVersionRegex
member.remarks:
Pattern:
clang version (?<full>(?<major>\\d+)\\.(?<minor>\\d+)\\.(?<patch>\\d+))
Explanation:
○ Match the string "clang version ".
○ "full" capture group.
○ "major" capture group.
○ Match a Unicode digit atomically at least once.
○ Match '.'.
○ "minor" capture group.
○ Match a Unicode digit atomically at least once.
○ Match '.'.
○ "patch" capture group.
○ Match a Unicode digit atomically at least once.

member.name: M:UnrealBuildTool.UEDeployIOS.VersionUtilities.PullApartVersion(System.String,System.Int32@,System.Int32@,System.String@)
member.summary: Pulls apart a version string of one of the two following formats: "7301.15 11-01 10:28" (Major.Minor Date Time) "7486.0" (Major.Minor)

member.name: M:UnrealBuildTool.UEDeployIOS.VersionUtilities.CalculateUpdatedMinorVersionString(System.String)
member.summary:
Parses the version string (expected to be of the form major.minor or major) Also parses the major.minor from the running version file and increments it's minor by 1.
If the running version major matches and the running version minor is newer, then the bundle version is updated.
In either case, the running version is set to the current bundle version number and written back out.
member.returns: The (possibly updated) bundle version

member.name: M:UnrealBuildTool.UEDeployIOS.VersionUtilities.UpdateBundleVersion(System.String)
member.summary: Updates the minor version in the CFBundleVersion key of the specified PList if this is a new package. Also updates the key EpicAppVersion with the bundle version and the current date/time (no year)

member.name: M:UnrealBuildTool.GDKExports.MakeMSGamingRuntimeTarget(UnrealBuildTool.TargetRules,UnrealBuildTool.TargetInfo,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary:
(Experimental and unsupported) Modifies the given target rules so that it will produce a build that is compatible with PC GDK. Expected for Win64 TargetInfo only.
If using bUseCustomConfig=true, it is necessary to add the following entries to {project dir}/Config/Windows/Custom/MSGameStore/WindowsEngine.ini Otherwise, when bUseCustomConfig=false, add these to {project dir}/Config/Windows/WindowsEngine.ini
; (required) this is required to make BuildCookRun generate, deploy and launch msixvc packages [/Script/WindowsTargetPlatform.WindowsTargetSettings] CustomDeployment=MSGameStore
; (recommended) add this if you are expecting to do intelligent delivery and use on-demand chunks etc [StreamingInstall] DefaultProviderName=GDKPackageChunkInstall
; (recommended) add this if you are planning on using Xbox cross-platform save games [PlatformFeatures] SaveGameSystemModule=GDKSaveGameSystem
; (optional) set this if you are using EOSSDK [/Script/MSGamingSupport.MSGamingSettings] +AllowedMissingSymbolFiles="EOSSDK-Win64-Shipping.DLL"
; (optional) set this if you use bWithOSS=true [OnlineSubsystem] DefaultPlatformService=GDK
; (optional) only need the following sections if you use bWithPlayFab=true [/Script/Engine.Engine] !NetDriverDefinitions=ClearArray +NetDriverDefinitions=(DefName="GameNetDriver",DriverClassName="/Script/PlayFabParty.PlayFabPartyNetDriver",DriverClassNameFallback="/Script/PlayFabParty.PlayFabPartyNetDriver") +NetDriverDefinitions=(DefName="BeaconNetDriver",DriverClassName="/Script/PlayFabParty.PlayFabPartyNetDriver",DriverClassNameFallback="/Script/PlayFabParty.PlayFabPartyNetDriver") +NetDriverDefinitions=(DefName="DemoNetDriver",DriverClassName="/Script/Engine.DemoNetDriver",DriverClassNameFallback="/Script/Engine.DemoNetDriver") [PlayFab] AppId={Your Hex PlayFab Title ID String Here}
If using bUseCustomConfig=true, it is necessary to add the following entries to {project dir}/Config/Windows/Custom/MSGameStore/WindowsGame.ini: Othewreise, when bUseCustomConfig=false, add these to {project dir}/Config/Windows/WindowsGame.ini
; (required) this is necessary to stage the game for Win64 because GDK is a restricted platform folder [Staging] +RemapDirectories=(From="Engine/Platforms/GDK", To="Engine") +RemapDirectories=(From="{project dir}/Platforms/GDK", To="{project dir}")
member.param: Whether you have put all MSGameStore config settings in Config/Custom/MSGameStore/*.ini - typically done with a custom MSG build target MyGameMSGTarget : MyGameTarget
Whether to include the OnlineSubsystemGDK plugin. Requires additional config settings
Whether to include the PlayFab plugin. Requires additional config settings
Whether to use XCurl instead of libCurl

member.summary: For each root location relevant to install dirs, look for the given key and add its subkeys to the set of subkeys to return. member.param: The subkey to look for under each root location
member.returns: A list of unique subkeys found under any of the existing subkeys

member.name: T:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules
member.summary: Windows-specific target settings

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.Target
member.summary: The target rules which owns this object. Used to resolve some properties.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.bSetResourceVersions
member.summary: If enabled will set the ProductVersion embeded in windows executables and dlls to contain BUILT_FROM_CHANGELIST and BuildVersion Enabled by default for all precompiled and Shipping configurations. Regardless of this setting, the versions from Build.version will be available via the BuildSettings module Note: Embedding these versions will cause resource files to be recompiled whenever changelist is updated which will cause binaries to relink

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.bSharedResourceFiles
member.summary: If enabled, resource files will be shared between Modular target dlls for non-formal builds rather than linking in different resources to each dll. Additionally the build will instead call cvtres.exe to convert a compiled resource .res file into an object up front rather than offloading to link.exe, This is an optimization for Modular builds to prevent reprocessing the same resource for every dll which can be slow and spawn two additional processes per link.exe

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.bIgnoreStalePGOData
member.summary: If -PGOOptimize is specified but the linker flags have changed since the last -PGOProfile, this will emit a warning and build without PGO instead of failing during link with LNK1268.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.bUseFastGenProfile
member.summary: If specified along with -PGOProfile, then /FASTGENPROFILE will be used instead of /GENPROFILE. This usually means that the PGO data is generated faster, but the resulting data may not yield as efficient optimizations during -PGOOptimize

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.PreMergedPgdFilename
member.summary: If specified along with -PGOOptimize, will use the specified per-merged pgd file instead of the usual pgd file with loose pgc files.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.bPGONoExtraCounters
member.summary: If specified along with -PGOProfile, prevent the usage of extra counters. Please note that by default /FASTGENPROFILE doesnt use extra counters

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.bSampleBasedPGO
member.summary: If specified along with -PGOProfile, use sample-based PGO instead of instrumented. Currently Intel oneAPI 2024.0+ only.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.InlineFunctionExpansionLevel
member.summary: Which level to use for Inline Function Expansion when TargetRules.bUseInlining is enabled

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.bDynamicDebugging
member.summary: If specificed Visual Studio Dynamic Debugging support will be enabled, unless the compiler or linker is not MSVC or LTCG is enabled

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.Compiler
member.summary: Version of the compiler toolchain to use on Windows platform. A value of "default" will be changed to a specific version at UBT start up.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.ToolChain
member.summary: Version of the toolchain to use on Windows platform when a non-msvc Compiler is in use, to locate include paths etc.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.PreferredCompilers
member.summary: Order of preference when Compiler is set to Default. If populated and none listed are available this will cause a warning.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.DefaultPreferredCompilers
member.summary: Default order of preference when Compiler is set to Default. If populated and none listed are available this will cause a warning.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.Architecture
member.summary: Architecture of Target.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.ToolchainVersionWarningLevel
member.summary: Warning level when reporting toolchains that are not in the preferred version list

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.CompilerVersion
member.summary: The specific compiler version to use. This may be a specific version number (for example, "14.13.26128"), the string "Latest" to select the newest available version, or the string "Preview" to select the newest available preview version. By default, and if it is available, we use the toolchain version indicated by WindowsPlatform.DefaultToolChainVersion (otherwise, we use the latest version).

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.ToolchainVersion
member.summary: The specific msvc toolchain version to use if the compiler is not msvc. This may be a specific version number (for example, "14.13.26128"), the string "Latest" to select the newest available version, or the string "Preview" to select the newest available preview version. By default, and if it is available, we use the toolchain version indicated by WindowsPlatform.DefaultToolChainVersion (otherwise, we use the latest version).

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.bVCFastFail
member.summary: True if /fastfail should be passed to the msvc compiler and linker

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.bVCExtendedWarningInfo
member.summary: True if /d2ExtendedWarningInfo should be passed to the compiler and /d2:-ExtendedWarningInfo to the linker

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.bClangStandaloneDebug
member.summary: True if optimizations to reduce the size of debug information should be disabled. Disabling this option can decrease pdb size but may degrade the debugging experience. See https://clang.llvm.org/docs/UsersManual.html#cmdoption-fstandalone-debug for more information

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.bAllowClangLinker
member.summary: True if we should use the Clang linker (LLD) when we are compiling with Clang or Intel oneAPI, otherwise we use the MSVC linker.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.bAllowRadLinker
member.summary: True if we should use the Rad linker

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.WindowsSdkVersion
member.summary: The specific Windows SDK version to use. This may be a specific version number (for example, "8.1", "10.0" or "10.0.10150.0"), or the string "Latest", to select the newest available version. By default, and if it is available, we use the Windows SDK version indicated by WindowsPlatform.DefaultWindowsSdkVersion (otherwise, we use the latest version).

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.TargetWindowsVersion
member.summary: Value for the WINVER macro, defining the minimum supported Windows version.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.TargetWindowsMinorVersion
member.summary: Value for the NTDDI_VERSION macro, defining the minimum supported Windows version. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/winprog/using-the-windows-headers?redirectedfrom=MSDN#macros-for-conditional-declarations

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.bPixProfilingEnabled
member.summary: Enable PIX debugging (automatically disabled in Shipping and Test configs)

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.bUseWindowsSDK10
member.summary: Enable building with the Win10 SDK instead of the older Win8.1 SDK

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.bUseCPPWinRT
member.summary: Enable building with the C++/WinRT language projection

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.RayTracingMode
member.summary: Enables runtime ray tracing support.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.CompanyName
member.summary: The name of the company (author, provider) that created the project.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.CopyrightNotice
member.summary: The project's copyright and/or trademark notices.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.ProductName
member.summary: The product name.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.bEnableAddressSanitizer
member.summary: Enables address sanitizer (ASan)

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.bEnableUndefinedBehaviorSanitizer
member.summary: Enables undefined behavior sanitizer (UBSan)

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.bEnableAutoRTFMSanitizer
member.summary: Enables AutoRTFM sanitizer (AutoRTFMSan)

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.bEnableInstrumentation
member.summary: Enables instrumentation.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.bEnableLibFuzzer
member.summary: Enables LibFuzzer.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.bWriteSarif
member.summary: Whether .sarif files containing errors and warnings are written alongside each .obj, if supported

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.ObjSrcMapFile
member.summary: Whether we should export a file containing .obj to source file mappings.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.LinkReproDir
member.summary: Whether to have the linker or library tool to generate a link repro in the specified directory See https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cpp/build/reference/linkrepro for more information

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.ModuleDefinitionFile
member.summary: Provides a Module Definition File (.def) to the linker to describe various attributes of a DLL. Necessary when exporting functions by ordinal values instead of by name.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.ManifestFile
member.summary: Specifies the path to a manifest file for the linker to embed. Defaults to the manifest in Engine/Build/Windows/Resources.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.ApplicationIcon
member.summary: Specifies the path to an .ico file to use as the appliction icon. Can be assigned to null and will default to Engine/Build/Windows/Resources/Default.ico for engine targets or Build/Windows/Application.ico for projects.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.bDefineIconResource
member.summary: Whether to define or not the resource icon to be used in Default.rc2.

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.bStrictConformanceMode
member.summary: Enables strict standard conformance mode (/permissive-).

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.bUpdatedCPPMacro
member.summary: Enables updated __cplusplus macro (/Zc:__cplusplus).

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.bStrictInlineConformance
member.summary: Enables inline conformance (Remove unreferenced COMDAT) (/Zc:inline).

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.bStrictPreprocessorConformance
member.summary: Enables preprocessor conformance (/Zc:preprocessor).

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.bStrictEnumTypesConformance
member.summary: Enables enum types conformance (/Zc:enumTypes).

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.bStrictODRViolationConformance
member.summary: Enables enforcing standard C++ ODR violations (/Zc:checkGwOdr).

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.bDisableVolatileMetadata
member.summary: Volatile Metadata is enabled by default and improves x64 emulation on arm64, but may come at a small performance cost (/volatileMetadata-).

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.StructMemberAlignment
member.summary: Controls how the members of a structure are packed into memory and specifies the same packing for all structures in a module. See https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cpp/build/reference/zp-struct-member-alignment

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.bStripPrivateSymbols
member.summary: Whether to request the linker create a stripped pdb file as part of the build. If enabled the full debug pdb will have the extension .full.pdb

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.bNoLinkerDebugInfo
member.summary: If you supply -NoDebugInfo, windows platforms still create debug info while linking. Set this to true to not create debug info while linking in this circumstance

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.PdbPageSize
member.summary: Set page size to allow for larger than 4GB PDBs to be generated by the msvc linker. Will default to 16384 for monolithic editor builds. Values should be a power of two such as 4096, 8192, 16384, or 32768

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.PdbAlternatePath
member.summary: Specify an alternate location for the PDB file. This option does not change the location of the generated PDB file, it changes the name that is embedded into the executable. Path can contain %_PDB% which will be expanded to the original PDB file name of the target, without the directory. See https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cpp/build/reference/pdbaltpath-use-alternate-pdb-path

members.member: VS2015 updated some of the CRT definitions but not all of the Windows SDK has been updated to match. Microsoft provides legacy_stdio_definitions library to enable building with VS2015 until they fix everything up.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.DefaultStackSize
member.summary: The stack size when linking

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.DefaultStackSizeCommit
member.summary: The stack size to commit when linking

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.bForceHighDPIInGameMode
member.summary: Whether the embedded manifest will indicate the application is DPI aware

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.MaxNumTlsSlots
member.summary: Max number of slots FWindowsPlatformTLS::AllocTlsSlot can allocate.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.MaxNumThreadsWithTlsSlots
member.summary: Max number threads that can use FWindowsPlatformTLS at one time.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.PCHMemoryAllocationFactor
member.summary: Determines the amount of memory that the compiler allocates to construct precompiled headers (/Zm).

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.AdditionalLinkerOptions
member.summary: Allow the target to specify extra options for linking that aren't otherwise noted here

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.bCreateHotPatchableImage
member.summary: Create an image that can be hot patched (/FUNCTIONPADMIN)

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.bStripUnreferencedSymbols
member.summary: Strip unreferenced symbols (/OPT:REF)

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.bMergeIdenticalCOMDATs
member.summary: Merge identical COMDAT sections together (/OPT:ICF)

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.bOptimizeGlobalData
member.summary: Whether to put global symbols in their own sections (/Gw), allowing the linker to discard any that are unused.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.bSyntaxCheckOnly
member.summary: Tells the compiler to check only the syntax of the source files on the command line. (/Zs)

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.bReducedOptimizeHugeFunctions
member.summary: Whether to reduce optimizations for huge functions over an instruction threshold to improve compile time https://devblogs.microsoft.com/cppblog/msvc-backend-updates-in-visual-studio-2019-versions-16-3-and-16-4/

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.ReducedOptimizeHugeFunctionsThreshold
member.summary: The instruction threshold to use when reducing optimizations for huge functions, default 20000.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.bClangTimeTrace
member.summary: (Experimental) Appends the -ftime-trace argument to the command line for Clang to output a JSON file containing a timeline for the compile. See http://aras-p.info/blog/2019/01/16/time-trace-timeline-flame-chart-profiler-for-Clang/ for more info.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.bCompilerTrace
member.summary: Outputs compile timing information so that it can be analyzed.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.WindowsTargetRules.bShowIncludes

member.name: M:UnrealBuildTool.VCToolChain.GetVCIncludePaths(UnrealBuildTool.UnrealTargetPlatform,UnrealBuildTool.WindowsCompiler,UnrealBuildTool.WindowsCompiler,System.String,System.String,Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger)

member.name: M:UnrealBuildTool.VCToolChain.GetSxSIntermediateManifest(UnrealBuildTool.ReadOnlyTargetRules,EpicGames.Core.DirectoryReference)
member.summary: Get the intermediate location to store the side-by-side assembly manifest file for the given target.
member.remarks:
In theory, each target could have different dependencies, so ideally each target would have their own private assembly with its own copy of the dependencies. Unfortunately, the destination of the dependencies is scattered across various different build files (e.g. `Engine/Source/ThirdParty/Intel/TBB/IntelTBB.Build.cs`) which each specify that the dependencies should be output to a folder named for the target architecture. This means that it's impractical to output a unique assembly per target.
Instead, we can have binaries that are output to the same directory share a manifest, which ensures that all of all dependencies will be satisfied (though each application may get a few extra DLLs that they don't load). We only ever add to the manifests, because it's not safe to delete things - we can't force all targets sharing an output directory to build at the same time, and if targets A and B share an output directory, A has dep A' and B has dep B', then we would make the manifest unusable for B if we cleared it of B' when building A and not B. This does mean that we never remove dependencies that have been removed from the targets, but this is the safer end of the compromise.
