member.summary: Additional configuration related to Visual Studio user file (.vcxproj.user).

member.name: M:UnrealBuildTool.PlatformProjectGenerator.VisualStudioUserFileSettings.PatchProperty(System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary:
Patch existing .vcxproj.user file based on the new file content and corresponding patching settings, by doing the following: - Go over every <PropertyGroup> in the new document. - Finds <PropertyGroup> in the current document with matching "Condition" attribute. - If no property group is found, appends property group from new document to the end of current document. - Otherwise, saves values of properties configured with bPreserveExistingValue == true. - Removes all properties flagged for patching from current document property group. - Adds all properties required for patching from new document property group as declared in new document order.
This makes possible to override some properties crucial for UE features while keeping rest of user configuration intact.
Otherwise, if .vcxproj.user doesn't exist, patching do nothing and instead creates a new file based on new file content.
member.param: Name of the property to patch.
If true, the value of the property will be taken from existing user file, but relative order will be taken from newly generated file.

member.returns: The string to append to the user file

member.summary: Compares two project files ignoring whitespaces, case and GUIDs.
member.remarks: Compares two specified String objects by evaluating the numeric values of the corresponding Char objects in each string. Only space and tabulation characters are ignored. Ignores leading whitespaces at the beginning of each line and differences in whitespace sequences between matching non-whitespace sub-strings.
member.returns: An integer that indicates the lexical relationship between the two comparands.

member.summary: Write project file info in JSON file. For every combination of UnrealTargetPlatform, UnrealTargetConfiguration and TargetType will be generated separate JSON file. Project file will be stored: For UE: {UnrealRoot}/Engine/Intermediate/ProjectFiles/.Rider/{Platform}/{Configuration}/{TargetType}/{ProjectName}.json For game: {GameRoot}/Intermediate/ProjectFiles/.Rider/{Platform}/{Configuration}/{TargetType}/{ProjectName}.json
member.remarks: * TargetType.Editor will be generated for current platform only and will ignore UnrealTargetConfiguration.Test and UnrealTargetConfiguration.Shipping configurations * TargetType.Program will be generated for current platform only and UnrealTargetConfiguration.Development configuration only

member.name: M:UnrealBuildTool.ProjectFiles.Xcode.XcodeFrameworkWrapperProject.GenerateXcodeFrameworkWrapper(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary:
Generates an Xcode project that acts as a native wrapper around an Unreal Project built as a framework.
Wrapper projects are generated by copying a template xcode project from the Build Resources directory, deleting any user-specific or build files, renaming files and folders to match the framework, setting specific settings in the project to accommodate the framework, and replacing text in all the files to match the framework.
member.param: The directory in which to place the framework. The framework will be placed in 'outputDirectory/frameworkName/'.
The name of the project. If blueprint-only, use the actual name of the project, not just UnrealGame.
The name of the framework that this project is wrapping.
The Bundle ID to give to the wrapper project.
The path to the directory containing the framework to be wrapped.
The path to the root Unreal Engine directory.
The path to the 'cookeddata' folder that accompanies the framework.

member.name: M:UnrealBuildTool.XcodeProjectXcconfig.XcodeRunTarget.ProcessScripts(UnrealBuildTool.XcodeProjectXcconfig.XcodeProject,UnrealBuildTool.XcodeProjectXcconfig.XcodeProjectFile)
member.summary: Write some scripts to do some fixup with how UBT links files. There is currently a difference between Mac and IOS/TVOS: All: - The staged files gets pulled into the .app for a self-contained app, created at Xcode build time, unless an envvar is set that tells this script to skip the copy. This allows UAT to potentially skip copying staged files before we stage ("BuildCookRun -build -cook -stage -package" can skip the copying until the -package step). Also we don't copy anything if this is the engine, no-project, build, and an Mac: - UBT will link executable next to .app - We will copy it into .app here, similar to iOS - However, during Archiving, the .app is created in a intermediate location, so then here we copy from Binaries/Mac to the intermediate location - Trying to have UBT link directly to the intermeidate location causes various issues, so we copy it like IOS does IOS/TVOS: - IOS will link to Binaries/IOS/Foo - During normal operation, here we copy from Binaries/IOS/Foo to Binaries/IOS/Foo.app/Foo - Note that IOS and Mac have different internal directory structures (which EXECUTABLE_PATH expresses) - When Archiving, we copy from Binaries/IOS/Foo to the intermediate location's .app All: - At this point, the executable is in the correct spot, and so CONFIGURATION_BUILD_DIR/EXECUTABLE_PATH points to it - So here we gneerate a dSYM from the executable, copying it to where Xcode wants it (DWARF_DSYM_FOLDER_PATH/DWARF_DSYM_FILE_NAME), and then we strip the executable in place

member.name: M:UnrealBuildTool.XcodeProjectXcconfig.XcodeUtils.GetSupportedMacArchitectures(System.String,EpicGames.Core.FileReference)
member.summary:
Returns the Mac architectures that should be configured for the provided target. If the target has a project we'll adhere to whether it's set as Intel/Universal/Apple unless the type is denied (pretty much just Editor)
If the target has no project we'll support allow-listed targets for installed builds and all non-editor architectures for source builds. Not all programs are going to compile for Apple Silicon, but being able to build and fail is useful...
member.param: Path to the project file, or null if the target has no project

member.param: Path to the source file on disk
Optional sub-folder to put the file in. If empty, this will be determined automatically from the file's path relative to the project file
member.returns: The newly allocated source file object

member.name: M:UnrealBuildTool.XcodeProjectLegacy.XcodeProjectFile.GenerateSectionsWithSourceFiles(System.Text.StringBuilder,System.Text.StringBuilder,System.Text.StringBuilder,System.String,System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Generates bodies of all sections that contain a list of source files plus a dictionary of project navigator groups.

member.name: M:UnrealBuildTool.XcodeProjectLegacy.XcodeProjectFile.GetSupportedMacArchitectures(UnrealBuildTool.XcodeProjectLegacy.XcodeBuildConfig,EpicGames.Core.FileReference)
member.summary:
Returns the Mac architectures that should be configured for the provided target. If the target has a project we'll adhere to whether it's set as Intel/Universal/Apple unless the type is denied (pretty much just Editor)
If the target has no project we'll support allow-listed targets for installed builds and all non-editor architectures for source builds. Not all programs are going to compile for Apple Silicon, but being able to build and fail is useful...
member.param: Build config for the target we're generating
Path to the project file, or null if the target has no project

member.name: T:UnrealBuildTool.CppConfiguration
member.summary: Compiler configuration. This controls whether to use define debug macros and other compiler settings. Note that optimization level should be based on the bOptimizeCode variable rather than this setting, so it can be modified on a per-module basis without introducing an incompatibility between object files or PCHs.

member.name: T:UnrealBuildTool.CppStandardVersion
member.summary: Specifies which language standard to use. This enum should be kept in order, so that toolchains can check whether the requested setting is >= values that they support.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppStandardVersion.Cpp14
member.summary: Obsolete C++14 standard. No longer allowed, code will not compile with this version.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppStandardVersion.Cpp17
member.summary: Obsolete C++17 standard. No longer allowed, code will not compile with this version.

member.summary: C++20 standard

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppStandardVersion.Cpp23
member.summary: C++23 standard. Experimental, compiler support is currently limited.

member.summary: Latest standard supported by the compiler

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppStandardVersion.Default
member.summary: Use the default standard version (BuildSettingsVersion.V1-V3: Cpp17, V4: Cpp20)

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppStandardVersion.EngineDefault
member.summary: Use the default standard version for engine modules

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppStandardVersion.Minimum
member.summary: The minimum allowed C++ standard version. Lower values than this will cause a build error.

member.summary: Supports C11

member.summary: Supports C17

member.summary: Latest standard supported by the compiler

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CStandardVersion.Default

member.name: T:UnrealBuildTool.MinimumCpuArchitectureX64
member.summary: Specifies the architecture for code generation on x64 for windows platforms. Note that by enabling this you are changing the minspec for the PC platform, and the resultant executable will crash on machines without AVX support. For more details please see https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cpp/build/reference/arch-x64

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.MinimumCpuArchitectureX64.None
member.summary: No minimum architecture

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.MinimumCpuArchitectureX64.AVX
member.summary: Enables the use of Intel Advanced Vector Extensions instructions

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.MinimumCpuArchitectureX64.AVX2
member.summary: Enables the use of Intel Advanced Vector Extensions 2 instructions

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.MinimumCpuArchitectureX64.AVX512
member.summary: Enables the use of Intel Advanced Vector Extensions 512 instructions

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.MinimumCpuArchitectureX64.AVX10_1
member.summary: Enables the use of Intel Advanced Vector Extensions 10 version 1 instructions

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.MinimumCpuArchitectureX64.AVX10_2
member.summary: Enables the use of Intel Advanced Vector Extensions 10 version 2 instructions

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.MinimumCpuArchitectureX64.Default
member.summary: Use the default minimum architecture

member.name: T:UnrealBuildTool.Arm64TargetCpuType
member.summary: Target ARM64 CPU for codegen. This defines different ARM ISA and extension targets

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.Arm64TargetCpuType.Default
member.summary: Default ARMv8.0-A ISA target CPU, supported by every 64 bit ARM CPU

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.Arm64TargetCpuType.Graviton2
member.summary: AWS Graviton2 CPU - ARM Neoverse N1 uArch with ARMv8.2-A ISA + FP16 + rcpc + dotprod + crypto extensions

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.Arm64TargetCpuType.GravitonSVE
member.summary: AWS Graviton SVE (Graviton3+) - targets Neoverse 512TVB with SVE + bf16 + crypto. Cross-generation target compatible with Graviton3, Graviton4, and future SVE-capable Graviton processors. NOT compatible with Graviton2 (no SVE support). For Graviton2, use the Graviton2 target. For full Graviton4 optimizations (SVE2, ARMv9.0-A), use the Graviton4 target.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.Arm64TargetCpuType.Graviton3
member.summary: AWS Graviton3 CPU - ARM Neoverse V1 uArch with ARMv8.4-A ISA + SVE + bf16 + rng + crypto extensions

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.Arm64TargetCpuType.Graviton4
member.summary: AWS Graviton4 CPU - ARM Neoverse V2 uArch with ARMv9.0-A ISA + SVE2 + bf16 + rng + crypto extensions

member.summary: If unity builds are enabled this can be used to override if this specific module will build using Unity. This is set using the per module configurations in BuildConfiguration.

member.summary: The number of source files in this module before unity build will be activated for that module. If set to anything besides -1, will override the default setting which is controlled by MinGameModuleSourceFilesForUnityBuild

member.summary: Whether to retain frame pointers

member.summary: Enable objective C exception handling

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileEnvironment.bEnableObjCAutomaticReferenceCounting
member.summary: Enable objective C automatic reference counting (ARC) If you set this to true you should not use shared PCHs for this module. The engine won't be extensively using ARC in the short term Not doing this will result in a compile errors because shared PCHs were compiled with different flags than consumer

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileEnvironment.DefaultWarningLevel
member.summary: How to treat any warnings in the code

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileEnvironment.CppCompileWarnings
member.summary: The compiler warnings associated with the compile environment.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileEnvironment.bWarningsAsErrors
member.summary: Whether to treat all warnings as errors

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileEnvironment.bDisableStaticAnalysis
member.summary: Whether to disable all static analysis - Clang, MSVC, PVS-Studio.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileEnvironment.bStaticAnalyzerExtensions
member.summary: Enable additional analyzer extension warnings using the EspXEngine plugin. This is only supported for MSVC. See https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cpp/code-quality/using-the-cpp-core-guidelines-checkers This will add a large number of warnings by default. It's recommended to use StaticAnalyzerRulesets if this is enabled.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileEnvironment.StaticAnalyzerRulesets
member.summary: The static analyzer rulesets that should be used to filter warnings. This is only supported for MSVC. See https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cpp/code-quality/using-rule-sets-to-specify-the-cpp-rules-to-run

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileEnvironment.StaticAnalyzerCheckers
member.summary: The static analyzer checkers that should be enabled rather than the defaults. This is only supported for Clang.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileEnvironment.StaticAnalyzerDisabledCheckers
member.summary: The static analyzer default checkers that should be disabled. Unused if StaticAnalyzerCheckers is populated. This is only supported for Clang.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileEnvironment.StaticAnalyzerAdditionalCheckers
member.summary: The static analyzer non-default checkers that should be enabled. Unused if StaticAnalyzerCheckers is populated. This is only supported for Clang.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileEnvironment.StaticAnalyzerPVSDisabledErrors
member.summary: The PVS Studio analysis warnings that should be disabled.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileEnvironment.bDisableDynamicAnalysis
member.summary: Whether to disable all dynamic analysis (such as Address Sanitizer)

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileEnvironment.bOptimizeCode
member.summary: True if compiler optimizations should be enabled. This setting is distinct from the configuration (see CPPTargetConfiguration).

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileEnvironment.bCodeCoverage
member.summary: True if the compilation should produce tracing output for code coverage.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileEnvironment.OptimizationLevel
member.summary: Allows to fine tune optimizations level for speed and\or code size

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileEnvironment.FPSemantics
member.summary: Determines the FP semantics.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileEnvironment.bCreateDebugInfo
member.summary: True if debug info should be created.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileEnvironment.bDebugLineTablesOnly
member.summary: True if debug info should only generate line number tables (clang)

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileEnvironment.bDebugNoInlineLineTables
member.summary: If true, omits line tables for inlined functions at their callsites, and only includes line information for the caller.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileEnvironment.bDebugSimpleTemplateNames
member.summary: If true, use simple names for templates in debug type info. i.e. just TArray without template parameters. Template argument information is still present in the type information.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileEnvironment.bIsBuildingLibrary
member.summary: True if we're compiling .cpp files that will go into a library (.lib file)

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileEnvironment.bIsBuildingDLL
member.summary: True if we're compiling a DLL

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileEnvironment.bUseStaticCRT
member.summary: Whether we should compile using the statically-linked CRT. This is not widely supported for the whole engine, but is required for programs that need to run without dependencies.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileEnvironment.bUseDebugCRT
member.summary: Whether to use the debug CRT in debug configurations

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileEnvironment.bOmitFramePointers
member.summary: Whether to omit frame pointers or not. Disabling is useful for e.g. memory profiling on the PC

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileEnvironment.bEnableOSX109Support
member.summary: Whether we should compile with support for OS X 10.9 Mavericks. Used for some tools that we need to be compatible with this version of OS X.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileEnvironment.bUsePDBFiles
member.summary: Whether PDB files should be used for Visual C++ builds.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileEnvironment.bPreprocessOnly
member.summary: Whether to just preprocess source files

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileEnvironment.bWithAssembly
member.summary: Should an assembly file be generated while compiling. Works exclusively on MSVC compilers for now.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileEnvironment.bSupportEditAndContinue
member.summary: Whether to support edit and continue. Only works on Microsoft compilers in 32-bit compiles.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileEnvironment.bUseIncrementalLinking
member.summary: Whether to use incremental linking or not.

member.summary: Whether to allow the use of LTCG (link time code generation)

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileEnvironment.bPrintTimingInfo

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileEnvironment.bAllowRemotelyCompiledPCHs
member.summary: When enabled, allows XGE to compile pre-compiled header files on remote machines. Otherwise, PCHs are always generated locally.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileEnvironment.UserIncludePaths
member.summary: Ordered list of include paths for the module

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileEnvironment.SystemIncludePaths
member.summary: The include paths where changes to contained files won't cause dependent C++ source files to be recompiled, unless BuildConfiguration.bCheckSystemHeadersForModification==true.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileEnvironment.SharedUserIncludePaths
member.summary: The include paths which were previously in UserIncludePaths, but are now in a shared response file, persisted in the environment for validation. Do not add to this set unless a shared response is in use, and only when removing those headers from UserIncludePaths.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileEnvironment.SharedSystemIncludePaths
member.summary: The include paths which were previously in SystemIncludePaths, but are now in a shared response file, persisted in the environment for validation. Do not add to this set unless a shared response is in use, and only when removing those headers from SystemIncludePaths.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileEnvironment.MinCpuArchX64
member.summary: Direct the compiler to generate AVX instructions wherever SSE or AVX intrinsics are used. Note that by enabling this you are changing the minspec for the PC platform, and the resultant executable will crash on machines without AVX support.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileEnvironment.bMinCpuArchAPX
member.summary: Direct the compiler to compile with APX support for X64. Note that by enabling this you are changing the minspec for the PC platform, and the resultant executable will crash on machines without APX support.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileEnvironment.MinArm64CpuTarget
member.summary: Select a minimum ARM64 target CPU. This will change what ARMv8(9)-A ISA + extension code will be compiled for. Changing it from default will guarantee not to work on all ARM64 CPUs. Currently supported by clang compiler only

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileEnvironment.AnalyzeStackSizeWarning
member.summary: The amount of the stack usage to report static analysis warnings.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileEnvironment.bEnableCoroutines
member.summary: Enable C++ coroutine support. For MSVC, adds "/await:strict" to the command line. Program should #include <coroutine> For Clang, adds "-fcoroutines-ts" to the command line. Program should #include <experimental/coroutine> (not supported in every clang toolchain)

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileEnvironment.IncludeOrderVersion
member.summary: What version of include order specified by the module rules. Used to determine shared PCH variants.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileEnvironment.bDeterministic
member.summary: Set flags for deterministic compiles.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileEnvironment.bValidateFormatStrings
member.summary: Emits compilation errors for incorrect UE_LOG format strings.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileEnvironment.bValidateInternalApi
member.summary: Emits deprecated warnings\errors for internal API usage for non-engine modules.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileEnvironment.bValidateExperimentalApi
member.summary: Emits deprecated warnings\errors for experimental API usage for non-engine modules.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.CppCompileEnvironment.bRequiresPlatformSDK
member.summary: Experimental: Whether this module requires platform specific SDK files. Not supported on all platforms yet.

Provides good string data obsfucation without the downsides of full package encryption, with the security drawbacks of still having some data stored unencrypted

member.name: P:UnrealBuildTool.EncryptionAndSigning.CryptoSettings.bEnablePakFullAssetEncryption
member.summary: Encrypt all assets data files (including exp and ubulk) in the pak file. Likely to be slow, and to cause high data entropy (bad for delta patching)

member.name: T:UnrealBuildTool.LoadOrderManifest
member.summary:
Manifest file (.loadorder) describing the order in which all dynamic modules should be loaded. For this reason, it's only relevant to modular builds on some platforms.
This file exists because on some non-Windows platforms dynamic modules (such as DLLs) don't support dependencies and it's our responsibility to load them in the right order (Windows DLLs know which other DLLs they rely on and these dependencies are guaranteed to be loaded first). If we fail respect this order, we're likely to get crashes e.g. when global statics are being initialized because some dereferenced globals may not be available yet (null).

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.LoadOrderManifest.Libraries
member.summary: List of all project's dynamic modules in the order in which they should be loaded. All paths are relative to the main binary's directory.

member.summary: Gets a filename for a load order manifest for a build with the given platform/configuration.
member.param: The modular app name being built
The architecture of the target platform
member.returns: Filename for the app receipt

member.name: T:UnrealBuildTool.ModuleManifest
member.summary:
Stores the version (or a unique build ID) for the modules for a target in a certain folder.
This allows the runtime to identify which modules are used for which files, and which version they're at. This prevents stale binaries from being loaded by the runtime when making a local unversioned build, and allows faster incremental builds than compiling the build changelist into every module when making versioned builds.

member.name: T:UnrealBuildTool.PluginSet
member.summary: Represents a group of plugins all sharing the same name -- notionally all different versions of the same plugin. Since UE can only manage a single plugin of a given name (to avoid module conflicts, etc.), this object prioritizes and bubbles up a single "choice" plugin from the set -- see `ChoiceVersion`.

Whether or not to make this the new prioritized "choice" plugin in the set.

member.name: T:UnrealBuildTool.HeaderUnitType
member.summary: The header unit type markup is used for multiple things: 1. To figure out if a header can be compiled by itself or not. Many includes are included in the middle of other includes and those can never be compiled by themselves This markup is used by both msvc header unit feature and IWYU toolchain 2. Tell if the header even supports being part of a header unit. If it does not support it will also prevent all includes including it from producing a header unit This is how to markup in headers to provide above info: // HEADER_UNIT_SKIP - Here you can write why this file can't be compiled standalone // HEADER_UNIT_UNSUPPORTED - Here you can write why this file can't be part of header units.

member.name: T:UnrealBuildTool.TopologicalSorter`1
member.summary:
Helper class implementing the standard topological sorting algorithm for directed graphs. It generates a flattened sequence of nodes in the order that respects all the given dependencies between nodes (edges). So, if we have edges A -> B and B -> C, A will be always before B and C, and B will be before C.
In many cases there are more than one possible solutions. This helper just generates one of them. The algorithm only works for directed acyclic graphs, as otherwise it's impossible to order the nodes. In this implementation we can choose how cycles should be handled (CycleHandling member). By default sorting fails, but we may set the class to arbitrarily break cycles and complete the task.

member.name: T:UnrealBuildTool.TopologicalSorter`1.CycleMode
member.summary: Enumeration indicating how graph cycles should be handled.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.TopologicalSorter`1.CycleMode.Fail
member.summary: Fail sorting on the first cycle encountered (the algorithmically correct solution).

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.TopologicalSorter`1.CycleMode.Break
member.summary: Break cycles at the point they are detected (allows the algorithm to always complete but may skip some edges).

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.TopologicalSorter`1.CycleMode.BreakWithInfo
member.summary: Break cycles at the point they are detected (same as Break but outputs warning logs with information about the cycles).

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.TopologicalSorter`1.CycleHandling
member.summary: Determines what should be done when a cycle in the graph is encountered.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.TopologicalSorter`1.Logger
member.summary: UBT's logger object. Necessary if we want to get diagnostics, especially when BreakWithInfo is used.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.TopologicalSorter`1.NodeToString
member.summary: Functor returning a node's name. Necessary for some logging diagnostics, especially when BreakWithInfo is used.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.TopologicalSorter`1.Nodes
member.summary: List of all graph nodes (each user-provided node T is wrapped in an internal GraphNode).

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.TopologicalSorter`1.Callstack
member.summary: When traversing the graph, it represents all the nodes visited in the current sequence. For instance, if we go from node A to K and then X, Callstack will store A, K, X. It's only needed for diagnostic purposes i.e. when we find a cycle we use Callstack to output all the cycle's nodes.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.TopologicalSorter`1.Result
member.summary: The resulting sequence of nodes (T, provided by the user) in the topological order.

member.name: M:UnrealBuildTool.TopologicalSorter`1.#ctor(System.Collections.Generic.List{System.Tuple{`0,`0}})
member.summary: Initialize the sorter object and create the internal graph representation. The list of all the graph nodes doesn't need to be provided because it's internally generated from Edges (union of all edges' Item1 and Item2.

member.name: M:UnrealBuildTool.TopologicalSorter`1.Sort
member.summary: Sort the graph to generate a flat sequence of nodes respecting all graph dependencies.
member.returns: True on success, false if failed (most likely a cycle encountered)

member.name: M:UnrealBuildTool.TopologicalSorter`1.GetResult
member.summary: Get the list of sorted nodes (valid after successfully calling Sort).

member.name: M:UnrealBuildTool.TopologicalSorter`1.Visit(UnrealBuildTool.TopologicalSorter{`0}.GraphNode)
member.summary: Process a single node.
member.returns: True on success, false if failed (most likely a cycle encountered)

member.name: M:UnrealBuildTool.TopologicalSorter`1.CreateGraph(System.Collections.Generic.List{System.Tuple{`0,`0}})
member.summary: Create the internal graph structure for the provided list of edges.
member.param: List of tuples representing connections between nodes

member.name: M:UnrealBuildTool.TopologicalSorter`1.ClearNodesStates
member.summary: Clear the state (in-progress, done) of all graph nodes.

member.name: M:UnrealBuildTool.TopologicalSorter`1.HandleCycle
member.summary: Member executed when a graph cycle is encountered.
member.returns: True if operation should continue, false if a cycle is considered an error

member.name: M:UnrealBuildTool.TopologicalSorter`1.GetNodesInCycle
member.summary: Returns all nodes being part of a cycle (call valid only if we've really detected a cycle).

member.name: M:UnrealBuildTool.TopologicalSorter`1.GetNodesInCycleString
member.summary: Returns a string with a list of names of nodes forming a graph cycle (call valid only if we've really detected a cycle).

member.name: T:UnrealBuildTool.TopologicalSorter`1.Mark
member.summary: Graph node's state wrt the traversal.

member.name: T:UnrealBuildTool.TopologicalSorter`1.GraphNode
member.summary: Internal helper representing a graph node (wrapping the user-provided node and storing internal state).

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.TopologicalSorter`1.GraphNode.Data
member.summary: User-provided node object.

member.summary: List of graph nodes we can visit starting from this one.

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.TopologicalSorter`1.GraphNode.Mark
member.summary: Traversal state (in-progress, done).

member.summary: Prefix used for all dynamically created Unity modules

member.name: F:UnrealBuildTool.Unity.PlaceholderFileSize
member.summary: Constant used to estimate file size for generated files
member.remarks: When sampled, the median size for a generated file going into a Unity module was ~7.3KB. 4KB is rounding down to the closest power of two, as generated code has been observed to compile faster than non-generated code of equivalent size.

member.name: M:UnrealBuildTool.Unity.FileLength(UnrealBuildBase.FileItem)
member.summary: Safe way to get file length

member.name: T:UnrealBuildTool.Unity.FileCollection
member.summary: A class which represents a list of files and the sum of their lengths.

members.member: The length of this file collection, plus any additional virtual space needed for bUseAdapativeUnityBuild. See the comment above AddVirtualFile() below for more information.

member.name: M:UnrealBuildTool.Unity.FileCollection.AddVirtualFile(UnrealBuildBase.FileItem)
member.summary: Doesn't actually add a file, but instead reserves space. This is used with "bUseAdaptiveUnityBuild", to prevent other compiled unity blobs in the module's numbered set from having to be recompiled after we eject source files one of that module's unity blobs. Basically, it can prevent dozens of files from being recompiled after the first time building after your working set of source files changes
member.param: The virtual file to add to the collection

member.name: T:UnrealBuildTool.Unity.UnityFileBuilder
member.summary: A class for building up a set of unity files. You add files one-by-one using AddFile then call EndCurrentUnityFile to finish that one and (perhaps) begin a new one.

member.summary: Constructs a new UnityFileBuilder.
member.param: The accumulated length at which to automatically split a unity file, or -1 to disable automatic splitting.

member.name: M:UnrealBuildTool.Unity.UnityFileBuilder.AddFile(UnrealBuildBase.FileItem)
member.summary: Adds a file to the current unity file. If splitting is required and the total size of the unity file exceeds the split limit, then a new file is automatically started.

member.name: M:UnrealBuildTool.Unity.UnityFileBuilder.AddVirtualFile(UnrealBuildBase.FileItem)
member.summary: Doesn't actually add a file, but instead reserves space, then splits the unity blob normally as if it was a real file that was added. See the comment above FileCollection.AddVirtualFile() for more info.
member.param: The file to add virtually. Only the size of the file is tracked.

member.name: M:UnrealBuildTool.Unity.UnityFileBuilder.EndCurrentUnityFile
member.summary: Starts a new unity file. If the current unity file contains no files, this function has no effect, i.e. you will not get an empty unity file.

member.name: M:UnrealBuildTool.Unity.UnityFileBuilder.GetUnityFiles
member.summary: Returns the list of built unity files. The UnityFileBuilder is unusable after this.

Given a set of source files, generates another set of source files that #include all the original files, the goal being to compile the same code in fewer translation units. The "unity" files are written to the IntermediateDirectory.
member.param: The header files that might correspond to the source files.
The environment that is used to compile the source files.
Interface to query files which belong to the working set
Base name to use for the Unity files
Intermediate directory for unity source files
Receives a mapping of source file to unity file
Receives the files to compile using the normal configuration.
Receives the files to compile using the adaptive unity configuration.
An approximate number of bytes of source code to target for inclusion in a single unified source file.

members.member: The merged XML program to run

member.summary: Returns the ordered list of INI files that should be merged for the given platform + project, matching the hierarchy used by C# build code (ConfigHierarchy / ConfigCache). This keeps UPL and C# readers in agreement on which files override which — including platform Base*.ini, Generated*.ini, Custom/{CUSTOMCONFIG}/*.ini, LimitedAccess restricted layers, platform extensions ({EXTENGINE}/{EXTPROJECT}) and the DataDrivenPlatformInfo IniParentChain.
